障害所得保険：スタンドアローンまたはライダーとして購入可能 ｜ TPD給付：生命保険証券に組み込まれるかライダーとして付帯
障害所得保険：月額で給付支払い ｜ TPD給付：一時金で給付支払い
障害所得保険：月額給付は給与の最大75%まで ｜ TPD給付：保険金額は給与に連動しない
障害所得保険：エスカレーション給付あり ｜ TPD給付：給付のエスカレーションなし
障害所得保険：部分障害給付あり ｜ TPD給付：部分障害給付なし
障害所得保険：据置/待機期間の選択肢あり ｜ TPD給付：所定の据置期間なし（ただし通常6ヶ月の待機要件あり）
障害所得保険：稼得所得/給与のある者のみ利用可能 ｜ TPD給付：生命保険の死亡給付にバンドルされるため、無就労者にも制限なし

sections.title: Computation Of Disability Income Insurance Benefit

paragraphs.text: Disability Income Insurance policy pays a monthly income to the insured upon his disability. Let us look at how it actually works, using the case of an employed individual. See Example 4.1. Example 4.1: Example Of Computation Of Disability Income Insurance Benefit Tommy Tang, aged 34 years, is an assistant accountant in a finance company. His monthly income is S$6,000. He wishes to purchase a Disability Income Insurance policy to provide maximum coverage. However, the maximum amount of monthly benefit that he can purchase cannot be 100%. Why? Insurers will allow the benefit amount to cover only up to 75% of the insured’s average income over the past 12 months immediately prior to his disability. A ceiling on the income to be covered may also be imposed and a self-employed person may be granted a lower benefit, The purpose of replacing only a certain percentage of the insured’s income is to ensure that the insured will not be better off financially from the benefit which he receives under his Disability Income Insurance policy, so that he will have the incentive to go back to work. Details of his policy are as follows: Male, Age Next Birthday 34 years old Monthly Income S$6,000 Monthly Benefit S$4,500 (75% of his monthly income being the maximum amount as allowed by Alpha Insurance Company and assuming that he has no other disability benefits). Escalation Benefit 5% per year Benefit Period Up to the age of 65 years next birthday Deferred/Elimination Period 6 months Monthly Premium S$125.50
paragraphs.textJP:
障害所得保険証券は、被保険者の障害に対して月額の所得を支払います。雇用されている個人のケースを使用して、実際の仕組みを見てみましょう。

例題4.1：障害所得保険給付の計算例
Tommy Tang（34歳）は金融会社のアシスタント経理士で、月収はS$6,000です。最大限の補償を提供する障害所得保険証券の購入を希望しています。しかし、購入できる月額給付の最大額は100%ではありません。なぜでしょうか？保険者は、障害発生直前の過去12ヶ月間の被保険者の平均所得の最大75%までしか給付額をカバーすることを認めません。カバーされる所得に上限が課される場合もあり、自営業者にはより低い給付が認められる場合があります。被保険者の所得の一定割合のみを代替する目的は、被保険者が障害所得保険証券で受け取る給付により財政的に有利にならないようにし、職場復帰のインセンティブを持たせるためです。

保険証券の詳細：男性、次の誕生日年齢34歳、月収S$6,000、月額給付S$4,500（月収の75%、Alpha Insurance Companyが許容する最大額、他の障害給付がないと仮定）、エスカレーション給付：年5%、給付期間：次の誕生日年齢65歳まで、据置/待機期間：6ヶ月、月額保険料：S$125.50

paragraphs.text: If Tommy becomes disabled (according to the definition of “disability” of the policy) and is out of work, he will receive the benefit amount every month after the Deferred/Elimination Period, as long as he is unable to work. Assuming he is disabled throughout the Benefit Period (30 years) (Benefit period 65 years – Age next birthday 35 years), he would receive a total of S$3,587,695 (S$4,500 x 12 x 66.43881), although he would have paid only five years of premiums amounting to S$7,530 (S$125.50 x 60). Premiums are waived upon disability.
paragraphs.textJP: Tommyが障害を負い（保険証券の「障害」の定義に従い）仕事を休んでいる場合、据置/待機期間の後、働くことができない限り毎月給付額を受け取ります。給付期間（30年間）（給付期間65歳 − 次の誕生日年齢35歳）全体を通じて障害状態であると仮定すると、5年間の保険料S$7,530（S$125.50 × 60回）のみを支払ったにもかかわらず、合計S$3,587,695（S$4,500 × 12 × 66.43881）を受け取ることになります。障害時には保険料が免除されます。

障害所得保険は、被保険者が全部障害を負い働くことができない場合に所得を提供します。「全部障害」は保険証券で定義されており、以下のいずれかの形態をとる場合があります：
▪ 自己職業障害（own occupation disability）
▪ 修正自己職業障害（modified own occupation disability）
▪ いかなる職業における障害（any occupation disability）
▪ 重度障害（severe disability）

（注：66.4388は年金終価係数であり、公式 [(1+i)^n-1]/i（i = エスカレーション給付率、n = 年数）から導出されます。本教科書巻末の表A1「年金終価係数表」も参照してください。）

paragraphs.text: Let us look at each of the definitions in turn.
paragraphs.textJP: それぞれの定義を順番に見ていきましょう。

paragraphs.text: Considering the insured’s occupation, total disability is defined as: “the insured’s inability to perform the material duties of his own occupation”
paragraphs.textJP: 被保険者の職業を考慮し、全部障害は以下のように定義されます：「被保険者が自己の職業の重要な業務を遂行することができないこと」

paragraphs.text: This refers to the insured’s own occupation at the time that the disability begins. For example, Penny, a pianist, had an accident which caused the severing of three fingers on her left hand, which, in turn, resulted in her inability to work as a pianist. Thus, she had met the above definition of “disability” and would be able to claim under her Disability Income Insurance policy.
paragraphs.textJP: これは障害が始まった時点での被保険者自身の職業を指します。例えば、ピアニストのPennyは事故で左手の3本の指を切断し、ピアニストとして働くことができなくなりました。したがって、上記の「障害」の定義を満たし、障害所得保険証券に基づいて請求することができます。

paragraphs.text: The policy will state the insured’s occupation, and the insurer has to be notified of any occupation change while the policy is in force. Some insurers specify that the insured must either be unable to work in the pre-disabilty occupation or any other occupation.
paragraphs.textJP: 保険証券には被保険者の職業が記載されており、保険証券が有効な間に職業の変更があった場合は保険者に通知しなければなりません。一部の保険者は、被保険者が障害発生前の職業またはその他の職業のいずれかで働くことができないことを条件とする場合があります。

paragraphs.text: Here “Total Disability” is defined as: “the insured’s inability to perform any gainful occupation or a similar occupation for which the insured is reasonably suited by reason of education, training or experience”
paragraphs.textJP: ここでの「全部障害」は以下のように定義されます：「被保険者が教育、訓練、または経験の理由により合理的に適している有給の職業または類似の職業を遂行することができないこと」

paragraphs.text: This definition is more stringent than ”Own Occupation” as it includes “a similar occupation”. Thus, if an insured manages to find a job which is similar in nature to the previous occupation after his recovery, he will not be able to meet the definition of “Total Disability”.
paragraphs.textJP: この定義は「自己職業」よりも厳格で、「類似の職業」を含みます。したがって、被保険者が回復後に以前の職業と性質が類似する仕事を見つけることができた場合、「全部障害」の定義を満たすことができません。

paragraphs.text: This is a more restrictive definition of Total Disability. It means: “the insured’s inability to perform any occupation”
paragraphs.textJP: これは全部障害のより制限的な定義です。意味は：「被保険者がいかなる職業も遂行することができないこと」

paragraphs.text: Consider Penny, the pianist, whose fingers were severed. Under the previous definition, she was totally disabled because she was unable to perform the duties of her own occupation. However, suppose that she is able to teach, i.e. prepare students for the piano theory examination. Under “Any Occupation” definition, she will no longer be considered as being totally disabled.
paragraphs.textJP: ピアニストのPennyを考えてみましょう。指が切断されました。前の定義では、自己の職業の業務を遂行することができなかったため、全部障害でした。しかし、例えばピアノの理論試験の準備をする生徒を教えることができるとします。「いかなる職業」の定義では、Pennyはまだ教えることができるため、全部障害とは見なされません。

paragraphs.text: The wording used may vary from insurer to insurer, and there are many hybrids in the insurance market, which may not be easy to classify. Therefore, it is important that you find out the exact definition as used by your insurer and be able to explain it clearly to your prospective clients.
paragraphs.textJP: 使用される文言は保険者によって異なる場合があり、保険市場には多くの混合型が存在し、容易に分類できない場合があります。したがって、保険者が使用する正確な定義を確認し、見込み客に明確に説明できることが重要です。

paragraphs.text: This definition follows the Long-Term Care Insurance definition where the insured is unable to perform at least three of the six Activities of Daily Living (ADLs) – washing, dressing, feeding, toileting, mobility, and transferring.
paragraphs.textJP: この定義は長期介護保険の定義に従い、被保険者が日常生活の6つの基本動作（ADL）のうち少なくとも3つ——入浴、着替え、食事、排泄、移動、乗り移り——を遂行することができない場合です。

paragraphs.text: The insurer uses this definition when the insured’s disability occurred during a non-working period (a period of time in-between jobs, where the insured is still covered under the plan). When a disability occurs during the non-working period, the benefit amount will not be based on the last drawn salary, but will be a smaller pre-determined amount as stated in the policy.
paragraphs.textJP: 保険者は、被保険者の障害が非就業期間（被保険者がプランの下で補償されたままの、転職間の期間）に発生した場合にこの定義を使用します。非就業期間中に障害が発生した場合、給付額は最終給与に基づくのではなく、保険証券に記載された固定金額に基づきます。

paragraphs.text: This is when the insured recovers from a total disability and can start to do some duties of his occupation, but at a salary which is at least 25% lower than the pre-disability salary. For example, Walter was paid S$2,500 a month as a warehouse worker. His work involved moving materials both with a mechanical forklift and by hand.
paragraphs.textJP: 部分障害とは、被保険者が全部障害から回復し、自己の職業の一部の業務を行うことができるが、障害発生前の給与よりも少なくとも25%低い給与で働く場合です。例えば、Walterは倉庫作業員として月額S$2,500を受け取っていました。彼の仕事は、機械式フォークリフトと手作業の両方で資材を運搬することでした。

paragraphs.text: After an accident, Walter’s spine was injured resulting in him having movement difficulties. Since then, he could operate only the forklift, but was not able to move the materials using his hands. As such, he was given a lower pay of S$1,800 (S$1,800 ÷ S$2,500 x 100 = 72% i.e. 28% less than his original pay). In this case, Walter was considered to be partially disabled.
paragraphs.textJP: 事故後、Walterの脊椎が損傷し、動作に困難が生じました。それ以来、フォークリフトの操作のみが可能で、手で材料を移動することはできませんでした。そのため、給与がS$1,800に引き下げられました（S$1,800 ÷ S$2,500 × 100 = 72%、すなわち元の給与より28%少ない）。

paragraphs.text: Partial Disability benefit may be payable to the insured who works in other occupation (not related to his own occupation) on a part-time or full-time basis, and which pays him a salary of 75% (or less) of his Pre-disability Earnings.
paragraphs.textJP: 部分障害給付は、被保険者が自己の職業とは関係のない他の職業にパートタイムまたはフルタイムで従事し、障害発生前の稼得額の75%（またはそれ以下）の給与を受け取っている場合にも支払われることがあります。

paragraphs.text: If the insured who has been receiving the disability benefits returns to work but suffers a relapse from the same cause within a specified period of time (usually 180 days), most insurers will waive the deferred/elimination period, and the benefit payments will re-commence immediately.
paragraphs.textJP: 障害給付を受けていた被保険者が職場に復帰したものの、所定の期間内（通常180日）に同一の原因で再発した場合、ほとんどの保険者は据置/待機期間を免除し、給付の支払いが直ちに再開されます。

paragraphs.text: In such a case, the claim will be treated as a continuation of the earlier claim for the purpose of determining the duration of the benefit payment period. Income Before Disability Income = S$2,500 After disability, income was reduced to S$1,800 (being 72% of pre-disability income).
paragraphs.textJP:
このような場合、給付支払い期間の期間を決定する目的で、請求は以前の請求の継続として扱われます。

障害前所得 = S$2,500
障害後、所得はS$1,800に減少（障害前所得の72%）。

paragraphs.text: Benefit period is the maximum period for which disability benefits are payable to the insured for any one episode of disability. It can be short term (e.g. five years) or long term (e.g. up to age of 65 years). The shorter the benefit term, the lower will be the premiums.
paragraphs.textJP: 給付期間とは、1回の障害エピソードについて被保険者に障害給付が支払われる最大期間です。短期（例：5年）または長期（例：65歳まで）があります。給付期間が短いほど、保険料は低くなります。

paragraphs.text: A Disability Income Insurance policy will start paying its benefits after the insured has been disabled for a specified period known as the deferred, elimination or pre-benefit period. The condition for a deferred period eliminates costly claims for disabilities which are only short term. Insurers may offer to the insured different deferred/elimination periods (e.g. 45, 90 or 180 days), or by number of months (e.g. three months).
paragraphs.textJP: 障害所得保険証券は、被保険者が据置期間、待機期間、または給付前期間として知られる所定の期間にわたって障害を負った後に給付の支払いを開始します。据置期間の条件により、短期間のみの障害に対する高額な請求が排除されます。保険者は、被保険者にさまざまな据置/待機期間（例：45日、90日、180日）、または月数（例：3ヶ月）の選択肢を提供する場合があります。

paragraphs.text: Insurers offer these various deferred/elimination periods to meet the different individual’s specific needs. For example, a self-employed person may need a short deferred period, as his income may be immediately affected by his inability to work. An employed person, on the other hand, will usually continue to receive his salary for some specified period (e.g. three or six months). As such, you should advise your prospective client to find out from his employing company or his employment contract to see how long his company will continue to pay him his salary in the event that he is disabled, so that he can select the right deferred period. This will help him to save on the premium, as the longer the deferred period, the cheaper will be the premium, since the duration and frequency of claims are thereby reduced.
paragraphs.textJP: 保険者は、個人のさまざまな具体的なニーズに応えるために、これらのさまざまな据置/待機期間を提供しています。例えば、自営業者は所得が働けないことにより直ちに影響を受ける可能性があるため、短い据置期間を必要とする場合があります。一方、雇用されている者は通常、所定の期間（例：3ヶ月または6ヶ月）にわたり給与を継続して受け取ります。そのため、見込み顧客に対して、勤務先企業または雇用契約を確認し、障害を負った場合に会社が給与の支払いをどの程度の期間継続するかを確認するよう助言すべきです。これにより適切な据置期間を選択できます。据置期間が長いほど保険料は安くなります。請求の期間と頻度が削減されるためです。

paragraphs.text: You can also advise prospective clients with financial constraints to purchase a policy with a longer deferred period, where the premium is affordable to them, instead of not having the cover at all. = Longer Benefit Premium Period = Shorter Benefit Period = Short Deferred Premium Period = Long Deferred Period Premium Premium
paragraphs.textJP:
また、財政的制約のある見込み客には、補償を全く持たないよりも、保険料が手頃なより長い据置期間の保険証券を購入するよう助言することもできます。

給付期間が長い = 保険料が高い ｜ 給付期間が短い = 保険料が低い
据置期間が短い = 保険料が高い ｜ 据置期間が長い = 保険料が低い

sections.title: Benefits Offered Under Disability Income Insurance

subsections.label: A
subsections.title: Eligibility Criteria For Payment Of Disability Income Insurance Benefit

paragraphs.text: To be eligible for the disability income benefit payment, the insured MUST: (a) keep the policy in force; (b) be employed or in in-between jobs when they become disabled; (c) still be disabled after the deferred/elimination period; (d) meet the definition of total or partial disability as stated in the policy; (e) not have reached the expiry age as stated in the policy; (f) not have resided outside Singapore for more than a certain period of time (usually not more than six months); and (g) not have other sources of income (e.g. payment from Work Injury Compensation Insurance), which when totalled up, is more than the benefits due to him under his Disability Income Insurance policy.
paragraphs.textJP:
障害所得給付の支払いを受ける資格を得るためには、被保険者は以下を満たす必要があります：
(a) 保険証券を有効に維持すること
(b) 障害を負った時点で雇用されているか、転職活動中であること
(c) 据置/待機期間後も引き続き障害状態であること
(d) 保険証券に記載された全部または部分障害の定義を満たすこと
(e) 保険証券に記載された満期年齢に達していないこと
(f) 保険者の事前書面承認なく、1保険年度内にシンガポール国外に所定の日数（通常180日）を超えて居住していないこと
(g) 労災補償保険からの支払い等、他の所得源との合計が障害所得保険証券に基づく給付額を超えないこと

paragraphs.text: The main benefit offered by Disability Income Insurance is the monthly cash benefit to replace up to 75% of lost income when the insured becomes totally disabled through an accident or illness.
paragraphs.textJP: 障害所得保険が提供する主な給付は、被保険者が事故または疾病により全部障害を負った場合に、失われた所得の最大75%を代替する月額の現金給付です。

paragraphs.text: The benefit will be paid regardless of whether the insured is hospitalised. However, his disability has to be certified by a registered medical practitioner2 in order for him to make a claim under his policy.
paragraphs.textJP: 給付は被保険者が入院しているかどうかにかかわらず支払われます。ただし、保険証券に基づいて請求を行うためには、障害が登録医師によって証明されなければなりません。

paragraphs.text: If the insured’s total disability continues after the expiry of the deferred period, the insurer will pay the monthly benefit at the end of each month. For example, if the policy is issued for a monthly benefit of S$1,000 (as stated in the policy schedule), this will be the amount that the insured will get for that month. If the period of disability is less than one month, the insurer will usually pay a pro-rata amount based on a daily rate of one-thirtieth (1/30) of the disability benefit.
paragraphs.textJP: 被保険者の全部障害が据置期間の満了後も継続する場合、保険者は毎月末に月額給付を支払います。例えば、保険証券が月額給付S$1,000（保険証券明細に記載のとおり）で発行されている場合、これが被保険者がその月に受け取る金額となります。障害の期間が1ヶ月未満の場合、保険者は通常、障害給付の30分の1（1/30）の日額に基づく日割り計算額を支払います。

paragraphs.text: The benefit ceases when the insured recovers and returns to work, either fulltime or part-time, and even to a different occupation. 2 A registered medical practitioner is a doctor who is qualified by degree in western medicine and is legally licensed to practice medicine and surgery in the geographical area of his country, but does not refer to a medical practitioner who is the insured himself, as well as his spouse, child or parent of the insured. Income Insurers usually insure up to 75% of income
paragraphs.textJP:
被保険者が回復し、フルタイムまたはパートタイムで、異なる職業であっても職場に復帰した場合、給付は終了します。

（注2：登録医師とは、西洋医学の学位を有し、自国の地理的領域で医療および外科を行う法的ライセンスを持つ医師を指しますが、被保険者本人、およびその配偶者、子、親である医師は含みません。）

所得について：保険者は通常、所得の最大75%まで付保します。

paragraphs.text: Partial Disability Benefit is also known as “Rehabilitation Benefit”. It provides a reduced benefit to the insured if he is able to return to work at a reduced income of 75% of his Pre-disability Earnings, or lower. This applies even though he has not fully recovered. The reduced benefit is computed based on the following formula: Pre-disability Earnings − Present Earnings X Total Disability Benefit Pre-disability Earnings
paragraphs.textJP:
部分障害給付は「リハビリテーション給付」とも呼ばれます。被保険者が完全に回復していなくても、障害発生前の稼得額の75%以下の所得で職場に復帰できる場合に、減額された給付を提供します。減額給付は以下の算式に基づいて計算されます：

(障害前稼得額 − 現在の稼得額) ÷ 障害前稼得額 × 全部障害給付額

paragraphs.text: Example 4.2 shows how the partial disability benefit is arrived at. Example 4.2: Computation Of Partial Disability Benefit Larry, a lecturer who earns S$8,000 a month suffered brain injury in a car accident. This caused him to be totally disabled for two years during which he received S$6,000 (75% of his Pre-disability Earnings) a month from his Disability Income Insurance policy. In the third year, he managed to find a job in a printing firm that paid him S$1,000 a month. In view of this, the insurer reduced the monthly benefit to S$5,250, calculated as follows: S$(8,000 – 1,000) X S$6,000 = S$5,250 S$8,000 Note: You can see that the total monthly benefit and reduced income after the partial disability is S$6,250 (S$5,250 + S$1,000). This is still lower than the original income of S$8,000 a month before the disability.
paragraphs.textJP:
例題4.2は部分障害給付の計算方法を示しています。

例題4.2：部分障害給付の計算
大学講師のLarry（月収S$8,000）は交通事故で脳損傷を負いました。2年間全部障害となり、障害所得保険証券から月額S$6,000（障害前稼得額の75%）を受け取っていました。3年目に印刷会社で月額S$1,000の仕事を見つけました。これを受けて、保険者は月額給付を以下のとおりS$5,250に減額しました：

S$(8,000 − 1,000) ÷ S$8,000 × S$6,000 = S$5,250

注：部分障害後の月額給付と減額所得の合計はS$6,250（S$5,250 + S$1,000）であり、元の所得S$8,000よりも依然として低いことがわかります。

paragraphs.text: The insurer will pay a pro-rata amount, based on a daily rate of one-thirtieth (1/30) of the partial disability benefit if the period of the partial disability is less than a month.
paragraphs.textJP: 部分障害の期間が1か月未満の場合、保険者は部分障害給付の30分の1（1/30）の日額に基づく按分額を支払います。

paragraphs.text:
Insurers encourage participation in rehabilitation programme by reimbursing an insured for the rehabilitation costs incurred. The benefit is limited to a per disability basis and may include items such as:
▪ training courses;
▪ medical aids (e.g. wheelchair, walking frame, crutches); and
▪ workplace modifications.
paragraphs.textJP:
保険者は、リハビリテーションプログラムへの参加を奨励するため、被保険者が負担したリハビリテーション費用を償還します。給付は障害ごとの限度で制限されており、以下のような項目を含む場合があります：
▪ 研修コース
▪ 医療用具（例：車椅子、歩行器、松葉杖）
▪ 職場の改修

paragraphs.text: The insured must first get the insurer’s approval in writing, before going ahead with any rehabilitation programmes, etc. The rehabilitation benefit may be payable in addition to the monthly disability benefit (e.g. up to three times the monthly benefit).
paragraphs.textJP: 被保険者はリハビリテーションプログラム等に着手する前に、まず保険者の書面による承認を得なければなりません。リハビリテーション給付は月額障害給付に加えて支払われる場合があります（例：月額給付の最大3倍）。

paragraphs.text: Escalation benefit is a benefit offered by insurers as a hedge against inflation. With an additional premium, the insured can opt for a 3% or 5% escalation benefit per annum.
paragraphs.textJP: エスカレーション給付は、インフレーションに対するヘッジとして保険者が提供する給付です。追加保険料を支払うことにより、被保険者は年率3%または5%のエスカレーション給付を選択できます。

paragraphs.text: This benefit is useful for long-term disabilities. Example 4.3 illustrates how the escalation benefit works. Example 4.3: Illustration Of The Working Of The Escalation Benefit Eddy, an engineer, became disabled on 30 June 2023. He was insured for S$6,000 (75% of his monthly salary of S$8,000) and had opted for a 3% per annum escalation benefit and a 6-month deferred period. The events following his disablement were as follows: Date Event 1-1-2023 Insurer made the first monthly payment of S$6,000 1-2-2023 Insurer made the second monthly payment of S$6,000 1-1-2024 Insurer paid S$6,180 [(S$6,000 + S$6,000 x 3% (escalation benefit)] 1-2-2024 Eddy had since found a job as a clerk which paid him S$2,000 a month. The insurer paid him a partial disability benefit of S$4,635 derived as follows: Pre-Disability Earnings – Present Earnings Disability Pre-Disability Earnings Benefit = S$(8,000 – 2,000) X S$6,180 S$8,000 = S$4,635 Eddy will continue to receive the Disability Income benefit until he dies or when the benefit period expires, depending on which is earlier. Eddy continued to be disabled Insurer continued to pay S$6,000 each month
paragraphs.textJP:
この給付は長期障害に有用です。例題4.3はエスカレーション給付の仕組みを示しています。

例題4.3：エスカレーション給付の仕組み
エンジニアのEddyは2023年6月30日に障害を負いました。月額給与S$8,000の75%であるS$6,000で付保され、年3%のエスカレーション給付と6ヶ月の据置期間を選択していました。障害後の経緯は以下のとおりです：

2024年1月1日：保険者が初回月額給付S$6,000を支払い
2024年2月1日：保険者が2回目の月額給付S$6,000を支払い
2025年1月1日：保険者がS$6,180を支払い [S$6,000 + S$6,000 × 3%（エスカレーション給付）]
2025年2月1日：Eddyはその後、月額S$2,000の事務員の仕事を見つけました。保険者は以下の算式で算出された部分障害給付S$4,635を支払いました：
(S$8,000 − S$2,000) ÷ S$8,000 × S$6,180 = S$4,635

paragraphs.text: The premiums due under the Disability Income Insurance policy will be waived during the disability benefit payout period (applicable to both total and partial disability). If the insured has paid the premium annually in advance, the waiver of the premium will effect from the next premium due date, even though the disability benefit payment has started. In other words, the premiums paid will not be refunded.
paragraphs.textJP: 障害所得保険証券に基づく保険料は、障害給付の支払い期間中（全部障害と部分障害の両方に適用）免除されます。被保険者が保険料を年払いで前払いしている場合、障害給付の支払いが既に開始されていても、保険料の免除は次の保険料支払期日から有効になります。言い換えれば、支払い済みの保険料は返還されません。

paragraphs.text: Most insurers provide a lump sum amount to be paid to the insured’s dependants, in the event of the insured’s death.
paragraphs.textJP: ほとんどの保険者は、被保険者の死亡の場合に被保険者の扶養家族に支払われる一時金を提供しています。

paragraphs.text: A limitation clause in the policies ensure that the insured will not be better off financially by claiming. The purpose is to prevent over-insuring by restricting the monthly benefit payable, so that the total benefit (including other Disability Income policies that the insured may have) shall not exceed the insured’s pre-disability earnings or some proportion of it.
paragraphs.textJP: 保険証券の制限条項は、被保険者が請求により金銭的に有利にならないことを確保します。目的は、月額給付を制限することにより過剰保険を防止し、総給付（被保険者が有する可能性のある他の障害所得保険証券を含む）が障害発生前の月額所得を超えないようにすることです。

paragraphs.text:
In the event of a claim, the disability benefit payable will be reduced by the following sources of income:
▪ payments from other insurance policies against disability (except the Total and Permanent Disability Benefit under Life Insurance policies), including Work Injury Compensation Insurance; and
▪ any continuing salary, commission or other incomes derived from the insured’s occupation or business in which he was engaged just before his disability.
paragraphs.textJP:
請求が発生した場合、支払われる障害給付は以下の所得源により減額されます：
▪ 労災補償保険を含む、障害に対する他の保険証券からの支払い（生命保険証券に基づくTPD給付を除く）
▪ 障害発生直前に被保険者が従事していた職業または事業から得られる継続的な給与、手数料、その他の所得

paragraphs.text: The amount of disability income benefit paid together with any of the above sources of income will at no time be more than the amount of monthly benefit insured under the policy. That is why it is important for the insured to select the right deferred/elimination period, especially if his employer will continue to pay him his salary even after he is disabled. If his employer pays him six months’ salary in the event that he is disabled, and the deferred/elimination period chosen by him is three months, the insurer will not pay him any benefits after his deferred/elimination period has expired, as long as the employer continues to pay him his full salary.
paragraphs.textJP: 支払われる障害所得給付の額は、上記のいずれかの所得源と合わせて、いかなる時点でも保険証券に基づいて付保された月額給付の額を超えることはありません。そのため、特に雇用主が障害後も給与の支払いを継続する場合、被保険者は適切な据置/待機期間を選択することが重要です。雇用主が障害時に6ヶ月間の給与を支払い、被保険者が選択した据置/待機期間が3ヶ月である場合、雇用主が全額給与の支払いを継続している限り、据置/待機期間が満了しても保険者は給付を支払いません。

sections.title: Features Of A Disability Income Insurance Policy

paragraphs.text: The features of a typical Disability Income Insurance policy are described below: (a) It can be issued as a stand-alone (for which then, no rider is possible) policy, or as a rider to a basic Life Insurance plan. (b) It provides a regular monthly income during the insured’s total and partial disablement. (c) The premiums are waived during the benefit period. (d) The benefits may be level, or may increase at a given rate. (e) There is a choice of deferred/elimination period. (f) There is a choice of benefit period. (g) There is a choice of escalation benefit (if available). (h) The level premium can be paid on a monthly, quarterly, half-yearly or annual basis, depending on the insurer’s practice. (i) There is payment of partial disability benefits if a person returns to work earning a lower income. (j) There is provision of rehabilitation expense benefit. (k) There is provision of death benefit. (l) There is no restriction on the use of the cash benefits paid. (m) Disability benefit may discontinue if the insured stays outside Singapore for a specified period. (n) The cover will be terminated if the insured resides outside Singapore for more than a continuous specified number of days, within any one policy year, unless the insurer grants the insured the continuity of cover (an additional premium may be charged). (o) The cover will be terminated if the insured is not engaged in any fulltime occupation or profession for a continuous period of a specified number of days, when he is not suffering from a disability (see Section 11 of this chapter for the other causes for the termination of a policy). (p) The policy is usually guaranteed renewable and non-cancellable by the insurer (the insurer has the right to adjust the premium rates if the insured changes his occupation to one which is of a higher risk) (q) There is no surrender value, i.e. the insured cannot return the policy to the insurer for cancellation in exchange for cash payment. (r) Assignment of the policy is not allowed. (s) The policy cannot be written as a third-party policy. (t) The benefits received are non-taxable. (u) The policy will lapse if the premium is not paid within the 30 days’ grace period.
paragraphs.textJP:
典型的な障害所得保険証券の特徴を以下に説明します：
(a) スタンドアローンの保険証券（この場合、ライダーの付帯は不可）として、またはベースの生命保険プランに付帯するライダーとして発行できます。
(b) 被保険者の全部および部分障害期間中、定期的な月額所得を提供します。
(c) 給付期間中は保険料が免除されます。
(d) 給付額は一定額とするか、所定の率で増額させることができます。
(e) 据置/待機期間の選択肢があります。
(f) 給付期間の選択肢があります。
(g) エスカレーション給付の選択肢があります（利用可能な場合）。
(h) 平準保険料は、保険者の慣行に応じて月払い、四半期払い、半年払い、年払いで支払えます。
(i) 職場に復帰したが所得が低下している場合、部分障害給付が支払われます。
(j) リハビリテーション費用給付の規定があります。
(k) 死亡給付の規定があります。
(l) 支払われる現金給付の使途に制限はありません。
(m) 被保険者がシンガポール国外に所定の期間滞在した場合、障害給付が中止される場合があります。
(n) 被保険者が1保険年度内に連続して所定の日数を超えてシンガポール国外に居住した場合、保険者が補償の継続を認めない限り（追加保険料が課される場合あり）、補償は終了します。
(o) 障害を負っていない状態で、被保険者が連続して所定の日数にわたりフルタイムの職業または専門職に従事していない場合、補償は終了します。
(p) 保険証券は通常、更新保証かつ保険者による解約不能です（被保険者がより高リスクの職業に変更した場合、保険者は保険料率を調整する権利を有します）。
(q) 解約返戻金はありません。
(r) 保険証券の譲渡は認められません。
(s) 保険証券は第三者名義では契約できません。
(t) 受け取る給付は非課税です。
(u) 30日間の猶予期間内に保険料が支払われない場合、保険証券は失効します。

paragraphs.text: The underwriting process for Disability Income Insurance is very similar to Life Insurance. The different underwriting tools for individual employees and the self-employed persons are described below:
paragraphs.textJP: 障害所得保険の引受プロセスは生命保険と非常に類似しています。個人の従業員および自営業者のためのさまざまな引受ツールを以下に説明します：

paragraphs.text:
Proposal form (also called Application form) plus:
▪ Notice of Assessment; or
▪ Central Provident Fund (CPF) Statement (six months) and “large amount” questionnaire if the annual benefit is more than a certain amount, e.g. S$60,000; and
▪ medical test report (e.g. medical examination report, etc.) based on the age and amount of disability benefit proposed.
paragraphs.textJP:
申込書（申請書とも呼ばれる）に加えて：
▪ 納税通知書、または
▪ 中央積立基金（CPF）明細書（6か月分）、および年間給付額が一定金額（例：S$60,000）を超える場合は「高額」質問票
▪ 医療検査報告書（例：健康診断報告書等）

paragraphs.text:
Proposal form plus:
▪ Notice of Assessment for the last three years; or
▪ audited company’s accounts for the last three years; and “large amount” questionnaire if the annual benefit is more than a certain amount (e.g. S$60,000); and
▪ medical test report (e.g. medical examination report, etc.) based on the age and amount of disability benefit proposed.
paragraphs.textJP:
申込書に加えて：
▪ 過去3年分の納税通知書（Notice of Assessment）、または
▪ 過去3年分の監査済み企業決算書
▪ 年間給付額が一定金額（例：S$60,000）を超える場合は「高額」質問票
▪ 提案される障害給付の年齢および金額に基づく医療検査報告書（例：健康診断報告書等）

paragraphs.text: The underwriter will process the proposal form in the same way as explained in later chapter of this Study Guide, except that he will be looking at the risk of the proposer (intending insured) being disabled through an accident or illness, rather than the risk of death.
paragraphs.textJP: 引受人は、本スタディガイドの後の章で説明するのと同じ方法で申込書を処理しますが、死亡リスクではなく、申込者（付保希望者）が事故または疾病により障害を負うリスクに着目します。

障害所得保険の引受上の考慮事項は、他の種類の健康保険と類似しています。引受人は以下の要素により注意を払います：
▪ 職業
▪ 給付額
▪ 給付期間
▪ 据置/待機期間

paragraphs.text: Occupation is a crucial underwriting consideration for two reasons. Firstly, some occupations have a higher risk of disability from accidents (e.g. lorry driver, people working on heavy machines in manufacturing unit) or illnesses (people working on tobacco factory) than others. Secondly, it is easier to return to work with some degree of incapacity in some occupations than in others. For example, not only is a manual worker more likely to be disabled than an accountant, but it is also easier for an accountant to continue working with an injury like a broken arm, as opposed to the manual worker. Thus, most insurers have a rating structure depending on the class of occupation. A typical rating structure is as shown in Table 4.2. Proposal Form Table 4.2: Rating Structure Based On The Class Of Occupation Class I Professions and occupations involving indoor work mainly of a sedentary (deskbound) nature, e.g. accountants, doctors, and lawyers. Class II Office-based occupations with significant travelling to sites or production lines, shop-based jobs involving minimal manual work and senior supervisory positions for light manufacturing companies, e.g. insurance representatives, tour guides, and journalists. Class III Occupations involving light skilled manual work, medical related occupations, shop-based occupations involving light manual work, e.g. bakers, deliverymen, and hawkers. Class IV Occupations involving semi-skilled or unskilled manual work of a medium to heavy nature, e.g. bus drivers, commercial airline crew, and armed security guards. Decline (uninsurable) Occupations involving very heavy manual work, highly specialised skills, dangerous conditions or highly variable income, e.g. construction workers, oil riggers, professional divers, timber loggers, musicians, and authors.
paragraphs.textJP:
職業は2つの理由から引受上の重要な考慮事項です。第一に、一部の職業は他よりも事故（例：トラック運転手、製造ユニットの重機で作業する者）または疾病（例：タバコ工場で働く者）による障害リスクが高いです。第二に、一部の職業では何らかの障害を抱えながら職場に復帰することが他の職業よりも容易です。例えば、肉体労働者は経理士よりも障害を負いやすいだけでなく、腕の骨折のような怪我をした場合に経理士が仕事を続けることは肉体労働者よりもはるかに容易です。そのため、ほとんどの保険者は職業の等級に応じた料率体系を有しています。

表4.2：職業等級に基づく料率体系
クラスI：主に座業（デスクワーク）の専門職および職業（例：経理士、医師、弁護士）
クラスII：現場や生産ラインへの頻繁な移動を伴うオフィスベースの職業、軽微な肉体労働を伴う店舗業務、軽製造業の上級監督職（例：保険代理人、ツアーガイド、ジャーナリスト）
クラスIII：軽度の熟練肉体労働を伴う職業、医療関連職業、軽度の肉体労働を伴う店舗業務（例：パン職人、配送員、屋台経営者）
クラスIV：中程度から重度の半熟練または非熟練肉体労働を伴う職業（例：バス運転手、民間航空会社の乗務員、武装警備員）
引受不可（Decline）：非常に重い肉体労働、高度に専門化されたスキル、危険な条件、または大きく変動する所得を伴う職業（例：建設作業員、石油掘削作業員、プロダイバー、木材伐採者、音楽家、作家）

paragraphs.text: Class I is standard rating while the other occupation classes pay extra premium. Those in hazardous jobs, such as motorcycle delivery drivers, or individuals without income, such as housewives, are usually not insurable.
paragraphs.textJP: クラスIは標準料率であり、他の職業クラスは追加保険料を支払います。バイク便の配達員など危険な職業の者や、主婦など所得のない者は通常、付保不可能です。

paragraphs.text: It is a key requirement for the insured to inform the insurer in writing of any change in his occupation within a specified timeframe of the change. The insurer reserves the right to impose a loading, reduce the benefit, or exclude any claim arising as a result of his new occupation, especially if it is more hazardous than the previous one. Failure to inform the insurer of the change in occupation entitles the insurer to repudiate any claim made under the policy, or to adjust the benefit payable.
paragraphs.textJP: 被保険者は、職業の変更から所定の期間内に、職業の変更を書面で保険者に通知することが重要な要件です。保険者は、特に新しい職業が以前の職業よりも危険度が高い場合、新しい職業の結果として生じる請求について、付加料率の賦課、給付の減額、または除外する権利を留保します。職業の変更を保険者に通知しなかった場合、保険者は保険証券に基づくいかなる請求も否認するか、支払い給付額を調整する権利を有します。

paragraphs.text: The underwriter will assess the proposed benefits to ensure they are reasonable for the specific occupation, nature of work involved, and stated earnings.
paragraphs.textJP: 引受人は、提案された給付が特定の職業、関与する業務の性質、および申告された稼得額に対して合理的であることを確認するために評価します。

paragraphs.text: The benefit period is the duration that the insurer needs to pay out the benefit. Underwriters may use this to determine the terms of acceptance.
paragraphs.textJP: 給付期間は保険者が給付を支払う必要がある期間です。引受人はこれを使用して引受条件を決定する場合があります。

paragraphs.text: The deferred/elimination period chosen helps the underwriter to determine whether there is any moral hazard involved. For example, if an employee opts for a short deferred/elimination period, the underwriter, before making a decision on the terms of acceptance, may wish to find out the period for which the employer will continue to pay the employee in the event of a disability.
paragraphs.textJP: 選択された据置/待機期間は、引受人がモラルハザードが関与しているかどうかを判断するのに役立ちます。例えば、従業員が短い据置/待機期間を選択した場合、引受人は引受条件を決定する前に、雇用主が被保険者に病気休暇給与を提供する期間を確認したい場合があります。

paragraphs.text:
The benefits under a Disability Income Insurance policy will cease upon the occurrence of any one of the following events:
▪ when the insured is fit to return to work;
▪ when death occurs; or
▪ when the benefit period has expired.
paragraphs.textJP:
障害所得保険証券に基づく給付は、以下のいずれかの事象が発生した場合に終了します：
▪ 被保険者が職場復帰に適した状態になったとき
▪ 死亡が発生したとき
▪ 給付期間が満了したとき

paragraphs.text: If the Disability Income Insurance cover is issued as a rider, the cover will cease upon the expiry of the basic plan, or when one of the above-mentioned events occurs.
paragraphs.textJP: 障害所得保険がライダーとして発行されている場合、ベースプランの満期時、または上記の事象のいずれかが発生した時点で補償は終了します。

paragraphs.text: Most Disability Income Insurance policies do not pay the disability benefit or waive the premiums if the disability is directly or indirectly due to: (a) self-inflicted injury, while sane or insane; (b) indulgence in alcohol or drug taking, unless taken under the directions of a registered medical practitioner; (c) invasion, strike, riot, civil commotion, war (declared or undeclared) or act of war or warlike operation; (d) illness or injury sustained while the insured is in the service of the armed forces of any country, except for reservist in-camp training during peacetime operation in accordance with the Enlistment Act 1970; (e) pregnancy or childbirth or any complication arising there from. However, the exception is in the case of pregnancy, where the disability continues for more than 90 days after pregnancy termination , the disability will be B E N E F I T P E R I O D deemed to have commenced 90 days after the termination of the pregnancy; (f) participation in any aerial activity, including parachuting and skydiving or travel in any type of aircraft, other than as a fare paying passenger on a regularly scheduled flight of a commercial airline; (g) sexually transmitted disease, or any disability, illness or injury or any condition or complication arising from or caused by (whether directly or indirectly) the human immunodeficiency virus (HIV), including Acquired Immune Deficiency Syndrome (AIDS) and AIDS-related conditions; (h) participation in any professional sports or hazardous sports, including racing of any kind on horse or wheels, mountaineering, rock climbing and scuba diving; and (i) any pre-existing condition (i.e. condition occurring before the payment of the initial payment). The above list of exclusions may differ from insurer to insurer.
paragraphs.textJP:
ほとんどの障害所得保険証券は、障害が以下に直接的または間接的に起因する場合、障害給付の支払いまたは保険料の免除を行いません：
(a) 正気であれ心神喪失であれ、自傷行為
(b) 登録医師の指示に基づく場合を除く、アルコールまたは薬物の過剰摂取
(c) 侵略、ストライキ、暴動、市民騒擾、戦争（宣戦の有無を問わない）または戦争行為もしくは戦争類似行為
(d) Enlistment Act 1970に基づく平時の予備役訓練を除く、いずれかの国の軍務中に被った疾病または傷害
(e) 妊娠もしくは出産またはそれらに起因する合併症。ただし、妊娠の場合の例外として、妊娠終了後90日を超えて障害が継続する場合、障害は妊娠終了後90日に開始したものとみなされます
(f) パラシュート降下やスカイダイビングを含む空中活動への参加、または商業航空会社の定期便の有料乗客以外としてのいかなる種類の航空機での移動
(g) 性感染症、またはヒト免疫不全ウイルス（HIV）に起因する（直接的または間接的を問わず）いかなる障害、疾病、傷害、状態または合併症（AIDS関連の状態を含む）
(h) あらゆる種類の馬またはホイールによるレース、登山、ロッククライミング、スキューバダイビングを含む、プロスポーツまたは危険スポーツへの参加
(i) 既存の病態（初回保険料支払前に存在した状態）

上記の免責事項一覧は保険者により異なる場合があります。

paragraphs.text: The Disability Income Insurance policy will terminate upon the occurence of one of the following events: (a) the policy has reached its expiry date; (b) the premiums due are not paid within the grace period; (c) the insured dies; (d) the insured reaches the expiry age as stated in the policy schedule; (e) the insured resides outside Singapore for an aggregate period of more than a specified number of days (e.g. 180 days) within one policy year, without seeking the insurer’s prior written approval for continuation of cover; or (f) the insured is not employed in a full-time occupation or profession continuously for a specified number of days (except if he is disabled).
paragraphs.textJP:
障害所得保険証券は、以下のいずれかの事象が発生した場合に終了します：
(a) 保険証券が満期日に達したとき
(b) 猶予期間内に保険料が支払われなかったとき
(c) 被保険者が死亡したとき
(d) 被保険者が保険証券の明細に記載された満期年齢に達したとき
(e) 保険者の事前書面承認なく、1保険年度内に所定の日数（例：180日）を超えてシンガポール国外に居住したとき
(f) 障害でない場合に、連続して所定の日数にわたりフルタイムの職業または専門職に従事していないとき

paragraphs.text: Group Disability Income Insurance is purchased by the employer for its employees as an employee benefit. It can be issued on a stand-alone basis or as a rider.
paragraphs.textJP: 団体障害所得保険は、雇用主が従業員福利厚生として従業員のために購入します。スタンドアローンベースで、またはライダーとして発行できます。

paragraphs.text: Insurers will only issue a Group Disability Income policy if the employer has a Group Term Life Insurance policy with them, or they may also issue it as a rider to a Group Term Life Insurance policy. CLAIM
paragraphs.textJP: 保険者は、雇用主が団体定期生命保険証券を有している場合にのみ団体障害所得保険証券を発行するか、団体定期生命保険証券のライダーとして発行する場合もあります。

subsections.label: A
subsections.title: Coverage Provided By Group Disability Income Insurance

paragraphs.text: The cover provided by a Group Disability Income Insurance policy works in the same way as an individual Disability Income Insurance policy. The benefits and exclusions are also the same. Hence, we will not repeat them here.
paragraphs.textJP: 団体障害所得保険証券が提供する補償は、個人の障害所得保険証券と同じ方法で機能します。給付と免責事項も同じです。したがって、ここでは繰り返しません。

sections.title: General Underwriting Principles For Group Disability Income

paragraphs.text: INSURANCE
paragraphs.textJP: 保険

paragraphs.text: The selection process in Group Disability Insurance policies is similar to that for the individual disability plans. As the group must have a Group Term Life Insurance policy before they are eligible for this disability policy, most of the underwriting criteria will have already been met. The additional factors that the underwriters will need to take into consideration are: (a) nature of the company’s business; (b) age of the individual employee; (c) exact nature of work of each employee; (d) salary of each employee; (e) benefit period applied for; (f) benefit amount applied for; and (g) claims experience of the group under the previous Group Disability Income Insurance policy.
paragraphs.textJP:
団体障害保険証券の選択プロセスは、個人の障害プランと類似しています。グループはこの障害保険証券の適格性を得る前に団体定期生命保険証券を有していなければならないため、引受基準のほとんどは既に満たされています。引受人が追加的に考慮する必要のある要素は以下のとおりです：
(a) 会社の事業の性質
(b) 個々の従業員の年齢
(c) 各従業員の具体的な業務内容
(d) 各従業員の給与
(e) 申請された給付期間
(f) 申請された給付額
(g) 以前の団体障害所得保険証券に基づくグループの請求経験

paragraphs.text: The above information will enable the underwriter to determine the occupational class that each employee falls under, and whether the benefit amount applied for is justifiable. The past claims experience will enable the underwriter to determine the overall terms of acceptance.
paragraphs.textJP: 上記の情報により、引受人は各従業員が該当する職業クラスを決定し、申請された給付額が正当であるかどうかを判断することができます。過去の請求経験により、引受人は全体的な引受条件を決定することができます。

paragraphs.text: Note that some insurers may issue this policy only for white-collar occupations.
paragraphs.textJP: 一部の保険者はホワイトカラー職種に対してのみこの保険証券を発行する場合があることにご留意ください。

subsections.label: A
subsections.title: Claims Procedure For Disability Income Policy

paragraphs.text: Claims procedures for individual or group disability income are similar. In the event of a claim, the insured must notify the insurer within a specified period of time (e.g. 60 days) from the date of commencement of the disability. Failure to comply with this requirement might cause the claim to be invalidated. However, the insured could avoid this by proving that it was impossible for him to notify the insurer within the specified period, and by demonstrating that he had tried his best to do so.
paragraphs.textJP: 個人または団体障害所得に対する請求手続きは類似しています。請求が発生した場合、被保険者は障害の開始日から所定の期間内（例：60日）に保険者に通知しなければなりません。この要件に従わない場合、請求が無効となる可能性があります。ただし、被保険者は、所定の期間内に保険者に通知することが不可能であったことを証明し、最善を尽くしたことを示すことにより、この事態を回避できます。

paragraphs.text: The documents that the insurer normally asks for are as follows: (a) claim form giving the personal particulars of the insured, as well as details of his occupation and disability; (b) claimant’s statement; (c) Clinical Abstract form (see Appendix 4A); (d) physician’s statement/doctor’s statement to be completed by the insured’s attending physician giving full details of his disability; (e) NRIC or birth certificate for verification of the insured’s age; (f) evidence of earnings in the form of income tax returns (IR8A form), copies of payslips for the 12 months prior to the commencement of disability, and letter from employer to certify that the claimant (insured) is not being paid during the period of disability; (g) letter from company certifying that the insured’s service has been terminated (if available); (h) copies of Medical Certificates; (i) all available Laboratory and Tests results; (j) a copy of the Police Report if the cause of the disability is due to an accident; and (k) a copy of the Incident Report (required to be submitted to the Ministry of Manpower if the incident occurs at the workplace).
paragraphs.textJP:
保険者が通常求める書類は以下のとおりです：
(a) 被保険者の個人情報、職業および障害の詳細を記載した請求書
(b) 請求者の陳述書
(c) 臨床要約書（Clinical Abstract form）（付録4A参照）
(d) 被保険者の担当医師が障害の全詳細を記入した医師の陳述書/医師の報告書
(e) 被保険者の年齢確認のためのNRICまたは出生証明書
(f) 所得の証拠として、確定申告書（IR8Aフォーム）、障害開始前12ヶ月分の給与明細の写し、および障害期間中に請求者（被保険者）に給与が支払われていないことを証明する雇用主からの書簡
(g) 被保険者の雇用が終了したことを証明する会社からの書簡（入手可能な場合）
(h) 診断書の写し
(i) 入手可能なすべての検査結果
(j) 障害の原因が事故による場合は警察報告書の写し
(k) 事故が職場で発生した場合は事故報告書の写し（Ministry of Manpowerへの提出が義務付けられている）

paragraphs.text: The insurer may request for any other relevant supporting documents or information not listed above, on a case-by-case basis.
paragraphs.textJP: 保険者は、上記に記載されていないその他の関連する裏付け書類または情報を、ケースバイケースで要求する場合があります。

paragraphs.text: Some insurers may set a deadline for the submission of the evidence of disability or may require the insured to bear the cost of the evidence (e.g. medical reports) used to support his claim.
paragraphs.textJP: 一部の保険者は、障害の証拠の提出に期限を設けるか、請求を裏付けるために使用される証拠（例：医療報告書）の費用を被保険者が負担することを要求する場合があります。

paragraphs.text: For long-term disabilities, the insurer may request, at specified times, evidence to prove that the insured is still disabled.
paragraphs.textJP: 長期障害の場合、保険者は所定の時期に、被保険者がまだ障害状態であることを証明する証拠を要求する場合があります。

paragraphs.text: If the insured resides outside Singapore while the policy benefits are payable, the insurer may impose some or all of the conditions as specified below: (a) The insurer must be notified of the change of residence within 30 days of the change. (b) The insurer has approved the new country of residence. (c) The insurer has determined that the evidence, which can be submitted to make or support a claim under the policy from the new country of residence, is of similar (or better) standard, in terms of quality and reliability, as compared to that which would be available if the insured were to remain in Singapore. (d) The insurer has determined that the expertise and facilities for the care, treatment and rehabilitation of the insured in the new country of residence are of a similar (or better) standard, as compared to those which would be available in Singapore. (e) The insurer reserves the right to require an independent examination of the insured by its preferred doctor as and when reasonable.
paragraphs.textJP:
保険証券の給付が支払われている間に被保険者がシンガポール国外に居住する場合、保険者は以下に指定された条件の一部またはすべてを課す場合があります：
(a) 居住地の変更から30日以内に保険者に通知すること
(b) 保険者が新しい居住国を承認していること
(c) 新しい居住国から保険証券に基づく請求を行い、または裏付けるために提出できる証拠が、被保険者がシンガポールに留まっていた場合に利用可能であるものと同等（またはそれ以上）の品質と信頼性の基準であると保険者が判断していること
(d) 新しい居住国における被保険者のケア、治療、リハビリテーションのための専門知識と設備が、シンガポールで利用可能なものと同等（またはそれ以上）の基準であると保険者が判断していること
(e) 保険者は、合理的な範囲で、自ら選定した医師による被保険者の独立した診察を求める権利を留保すること

paragraphs.text: If any of these conditions are not satisfied, the insurer reserves the right to suspend the benefits payable under the policy until such time that the insured returns to Singapore, and that the insurer receives satisfactory evidence to resume payment of benefits.
paragraphs.textJP: これらの条件のいずれかが満たされない場合、保険者は被保険者がシンガポールに帰国し、保険者が給付の支払いを再開するための十分な証拠を受け取るまで、保険証券に基づく給付の支払いを停止する権利を留保します。

paragraphs.text: Disability Income Insurance policy provides benefits that are not available under other types of policies. The regular monthly income replaces a portion of the insured’s lost income, when he is disabled and unable to work. It helps to ease the financial difficulties that the insured and his family may face in such a situation. The policy also pays a reduced benefit if the insured is able to return to work after a disability, but accepts a lower-paying job. This helps the family maintain a standard of living similar to what they have predisability in addition, since there are no specifications on how the income received should be used, it can be utilized for any purpose, including repaying an outstanding loan.
paragraphs.textJP: 障害所得保険証券は、他の種類の保険証券では利用できない給付を提供します。定期的な月額所得は、被保険者が障害を負い働くことができない場合に、失われた所得の一部を代替します。被保険者とその家族がこのような状況で直面する可能性のある財政的困難の軽減に役立ちます。また、障害後に職場に復帰したが低い給与の仕事を受け入れた場合にも、減額された給付を支払います。これにより、家族は障害前と同様の生活水準を維持できます。さらに、受け取る所得の使途に関する指定がないため、未返済のローンの返済を含む、あらゆる目的に利用できます。

paragraphs.text:
As this policy contains a Limitation Clause to prevent over-insuring, you must ensure that the deferred period and percentage of salary to be insured are correct, so that in the event of a claim, your client will not be subject to the Limitation Clause. Furthermore, you must ensure that the cover will continue for the whole of the insured’s working life. As such, a thorough fact-finding or advisory process is important. APPENDIX 4A ABC Life Ltd 21 Any Street, ABC Centre, Singapore 654321 Tel: (65) 6789 8181 Fax: (65) 6789 8282 Clinical Abstract Form Important Notes: 1. This form is required for the application of medical report from hospital/clinic and should be completed by the patient or the patient’s parent (if patient is below 21 years of age) or the patient’s next-of-kin (if patient is deceased) 2. For request of medical report from hospital, this form is to be submitted to the Medical Records Department of the hospital. Date (dd/mm/yyyy) _____________ To: Doctor-In-charge Dear Sir/Madam Name of Patient______________________________________ NRIC No.____________________ Re: Application for Medical Report I hereby authorise you to furnish ABC Life Ltd, 21 Any Street Centre, Singapore 654321, with a detailed medical report on the above-named patient. This report is required for insurance purposes. I agree that a photocopy of this form shall be as valid as the original. Yours sincerely _________________________ ____________________________________________ Signature of Patient Signature of Patient’s Parent/Patient’s Spouse/Next-of-kin1 (if patient is below 21 or is deceased) Particulars of Patient Name (as shown in NRIC) NRIC No. Address Particulars of Patient’s Parent/Patient’s Spouse/Next-of-kin (if patient is below 21 or is deceased) Name (as shown in NRIC) NRIC No. Address Relationship to patient DISABILITY INCOME INSURANCE CHAPTER IMPORTANT CONCEPTS DEFINITIONS/EXPLANATIONS What is Disability Income Insurance? It is sold as an income-protection product in that it helps to replace a portion of the insured’s income that he loses if he becomes incapacitated and unable to work as a result of an accident or illness, subject to certain conditions. Differentiate between Disability Income Insurance and Total and Permanent Disability Benefit How Disability Income Insurance works?
▪ Insurers will allow the benefit amount to cover only up to 75% of the insured’s average income over the past 12 months immediately prior to his disability.
▪ Most insurers also impose a ceiling on the income to be covered (e.g. S$15,000).
▪ Some insurers grant a lower percentage for the self-employed, e.g. 60% of the past 12 months’ income.
▪ The purpose of replacing only a certain percentage of the insured’s income is to ensure that the insured will not be better off financially from the benefit which he receives under his Disability Income Insurance policy, so that he will have the incentive to go back to work. Disability Income Insurance Total And Permanent Disability Benefit Provides an income replacement if the insured is unable to work. Serves to accelerate the death benefit payable under a Life Insurance policy, to alleviate the financial burden or hardship of the insured and his dependants. Benefits are payable on a monthly basis for up to a fixed number of years (such as five or ten years), or until the insured reaches a certain age (such as the age of 60 or 65 years). Benefit under the Total and Permanent Disability Benefit is payable in instalments, or one lump sum, depending on the terms and conditions as specified in the Life Insurance policy. Disability definition relates to the extent the insured can fulfil the duties of his occupation. Disability is defined to be total and permanent, such as loss of limbs or loss of eyesight. It can be purchased as a stand-alone policy or as a rider. It is usually incorporated into the Life Insurance policies in Singapore. Maximum sum assured is up to a specified percentage of the salary. Sum assured is not pegged to salary. Escalation benefit is available. No escalation benefit. Partial disability benefit is available. Partial disability benefit is not available. Choice of deferred/elimination period is available (e.g. one month, three months, six months, etc.). No deferred period as specified (but usually there is a 6-month waiting period requirement as proof of permanent disability). It is usually only available to working adults with earned income/salary. There is usually no restriction on non-working people, e.g. children and housewives, as it is bundled with the death benefit under a Life Insurance policy. IMPORTANT CONCEPTS DEFINITIONS/EXPLANATIONS Total disability Term “Total Disability” is clearly defined in the policy and may take one of the following forms: 1. Own occupation disability; 2. Modified own occupation disability; 3. Any occupation disability and 4. Severe disability. Partial disability When the insured recovers from a total disability to perform some major duties of his occupation, but at a salary which is at least 25% lower than the pre-disability salary. Recurrent disability (linked claims)
▪ The insured who has been receiving the disability benefits returns to work but suffers a relapse within a specified period of time (usually 180 days) from the same cause, most insurers will waive the deferred/elimination period, and the benefit payments will re-commence immediately.
▪ In such a case, the claim will be treated as a continuation of the earlier claim for the purpose of determining the duration of the benefit payment period. Benefit period • It is the maximum period for which disability benefits are payable to the insured for any one episode of disability.
▪ It can be short term (e.g. five years) or long term (e.g. up to age of 60 years).
▪ The shorter the benefit term, the lower will be the premiums. Deferred/elimination period
▪ A Disability Income Insurance policy will start paying its benefits after the insured has been disabled for a specified period known as the deferred, elimination or pre-benefit period.
▪ The longer the deferred period, the cheaper will be the premium, since the duration and frequency of claims are thereby reduced. Difference between “own occupation”, “modified own occupation”, “any occupation” and “severe disability“ definitions Own Occupation
▪ “The insured’s inability to perform the material duties of his own occupation” Modified Own Occupation
▪ “The insured’s inability to perform any gainful occupation or a similar occupation for which the insured is reasonably suited by reason of education, training or experience” Any Occupation
▪ This is a more restrictive definition of Total Disability. It means: “the insured’s inability to perform any occupation” Severe Disability
▪ This definition follows the Long-Term Care Insurance definition where the insured is unable to perform at least three of the six Activities of Daily Living (ADLs) – washing, dressing, feeding, toileting, mobility, and transferring. Eligibility criteria for payment of Disability Income Insurance benefit
▪ Keep the policy in force;
▪ Be working (employed) or in-between jobs when being disabled;
▪ Still be disabled after the deferred/elimination period;
▪ Meet the definition of total or partial disability as stated in the policy;
▪ Not have reached the expiry age as stated in the policy;
▪ Not have resided outside Singapore for more than a certain period of time (usually not more than six months); and
▪ Not have other sources of income (e.g. payment from Work Injury Compensation Insurance), which when totalled up, is more than the benefits due to him under his Disability Income Insurance policy. Types of benefits offered by Disability Income Insurance IMPORTANT CONCEPTS DEFINITIONS/EXPLANATIONS Total disability benefit The main benefit offered by Disability Income Insurance is the monthly cash benefit payable to the insured to replace his lost income (e.g. up to 75%), when he becomes totally disabled through an accident or illness. Partial disability benefit Partial Disability Benefit is also known as “Rehabilitation Benefit”. It provides a reduced benefit to the insured if he is able to return to work at a reduced income of 75% of his Pre-disability Earnings, or lower, even though he has not been fully recovered. IMPORTANT CONCEPTS DEFINITIONS/EXPLANATIONS Rehabilitation expense benefit Insurers encourage the disabled insured to participate in rehabilitation programmes. They will reimburse an insured for the rehabilitation costs incurred. Escalation benefit It is a benefit offered by insurers as a hedge against inflation. With an additional premium, the insured can opt for a 3% or 5% escalation benefit per annum. Waiver of premium benefit The premiums due under the Disability Income Insurance policy will be waived during the disability benefit payout period (applies to both total and partial disability). Death benefit Most insurers provide a lump sum amount to be paid to the insured’s dependants, in the event of the insured’s death. How to compute the partial disability benefit? How the escalation benefit works? Eddy, an engineer, became disabled on 30 June 2018. He was insured for S$6,000 (75% of his monthly salary of S$8,000) and had opted for a 3% per annum escalation benefit and a 6-month deferred period. The events following his disablement were as follows: 1-1-2019: Insurer made the first monthly payment of S$6,000 1-2-2019: Insurer made the second monthly payment of S$6,000 Eddy continued to be disabled. Insurer continued to pay S$6,000 each month. 1-1-2020: Insurer paid S$6,180 [(S$6,000 + S$6,000 x 3% (escalation benefit)] 1-2-2020: Eddy had since found a job as a clerk which paid him S$2,000 a month. The insurer paid him a partial disability benefit of S$4,635 How the limitation of disability benefit clause works? Purpose is to prevent over-insuring by restricting the monthly benefit payable, so that the total benefit (including other Disability Income policies that the insured may have) shall not exceed the insured’s pre-disability earnings or some proportion of it. In the event of a claim, the disability benefit payable will be reduced by the following sources of income: 1. Payments from other insurance policies against disability (except the Total and Permanent Disability Benefit under Life Insurance policies), including Work Injury Compensation Insurance; and 2. Any continuing salary, commission or other incomes derived from the insured’s occupation or business in which he was engaged immediately prior to his disability. Pre-disability Earnings - Present Earnings Pre-disability Earnings X Total Disability Benefit Pre-disability Earnings - Present Earnings Pre-disability Earnings X Disability Benefit S$(8,000 - 2,000) S$8,000 X S$6,180 S$4,635 = = IMPORTANT CONCEPTS DEFINITIONS/EXPLANATIONS Features of Disability Income Insurance
▪ Can be issued as a stand-alone (for which then, no rider is possible) policy, or as a rider to a basic Life Insurance plan.
▪ Provides a regular monthly income during the insured’s total and partial disablement.
▪ Premiums are waived during the benefit period.
▪ Benefits may be level, or may increase at a given rate.
▪ A choice of deferred/elimination period.
▪ A choice of benefit period.
▪ A choice of escalation benefit (if available).
▪ Level premium can be paid on a monthly, quarterly, half-yearly or annual basis, depending on the insurer’s practice.
▪ Payment of partial disability benefits if a person returns to work earning a lower income.
▪ Provision of rehabilitation expense benefit.
▪ Provision of death benefit.
▪ No restriction on the use of the cash benefits paid.
▪ Disability benefit may discontinue if the insured stays outside Singapore for a specified period.
▪ Cover will be terminated if the insured resides outside Singapore for more than a continuous specified number of days, within any one policy year, unless the insurer grants the insured the continuity of cover (an additional premium may be charged).
▪ Cover will be terminated if the insured is not engaged in any full-time occupation or profession for a continuous period of a specified number of days, when he is not suffering from a disability (see Section 11 of this chapter for the other causes for the termination of a policy).
▪ Policy is usually guaranteed renewable and non-cancellable (the insurer has the right to adjust the premium rates if the insured changes his occupation to one which is of a higher risk) by the insurer.
▪ No surrender value, i.e. the insured cannot return the policy to the insurer for cancellation in exchange for cash payment.
▪ No assignment is allowed.
▪ Policy cannot be written as a third-party policy.
▪ Benefits received are non-taxable.
▪ Policy will lapse if the premium is not paid within the 30 days’ grace period. Documents required for the underwriting of Disability Income Insurance for an individual employee, as well as for the self-employed person Individual (Salaried Employee) Proposal form (also called Application form) plus: 1. Computerised payslip; or 2. Certified letter from company (with company letterhead) or 3. Notice of Assessment; or 4. Central Provident Fund (CPF) Statement (six months) and large amount questionnaire if the annual benefit is more than a certain amount, e.g. S$60,000; and 5. Medical test report (e.g. medical examination report, etc.) based on the age and amount of disability benefit proposed. Self-employed Person Proposal form plus: 1. Notice of Assessment for the last three years; or 2. Audited company’s accounts for the last three years; and large amount questionnaire if the annual benefit is more than a certain amount (e.g. S$60,000); and 3. Medical test report (e.g. medical examination report, etc.) based on the age and amount of disability benefit proposed. IMPORTANT CONCEPTS DEFINITIONS/EXPLANATIONS Underwriting considerations for Disability Income Insurance:
▪ Occupation Occupation is a crucial underwriting consideration for two reasons: 1. Some occupations have a higher risk of disability from accidents (e.g. lorry driver), or illnesses, than others. 2. It is easier to return to work with some degree of incapacity in some occupations than in others. Typical rating structure as follows: 1. Class I. 2. Class II. 3. Class III. 4. Class IV. 5. Decline (uninsurable). Underwriting considerations for Disability Income Insurance:
▪ Benefit amount Underwriter will assess to ensure the benefits proposed are reasonable for the type of occupation, nature of work involved, and stated earnings. Underwriting considerations for Disability Income Insurance:
▪ Benefit period Benefit period is the duration that the insurer needs to pay out the benefit. Underwriters may use this to determine the terms of acceptance. Underwriting considerations for Disability Income Insurance:
▪ Deferred/ elimination period The deferred/elimination period chosen helps the underwriter to determine whether there is any moral hazard involved. When would the benefits under a Disability Income Insurance policy cease to be payable?
▪ When the insured is fit to return to work;
▪ When death occurs; or
▪ When the benefit period has expired. Exclusions commonly found under Disability Income Insurance 1. Self-inflicted injury, while sane or insane; 2. Indulgence in alcohol or drug taking, unless taken under the directions of a registered medical practitioner; 3. Invasion, strike, riot, civil commotion, war (declared or undeclared) or act of war or warlike operation; 4. Illness or injury sustained while the insured is in the service of the armed forces of any country, except for reservist in-camp training during peacetime operation in accordance with the Enlistment Act 1970; 5. Pregnancy or childbirth or any complication arising there from, except in the case of pregnancy, where the disability continues for more than 90 days after the termination of pregnancy, the disability will be deemed to have commenced 90 days after the termination of the pregnancy; 6. Participation in any aerial activity, including parachuting and sky-diving or travel in any type of aircraft, other than as a fare paying passenger on a regularly scheduled flight of a commercial airline; 7. Sexually transmitted disease, or any disability, illness or injury or any condition or complication arising from or caused by (whether directly or indirectly) the human immunodeficiency virus (HIV), including Acquired Immune Deficiency Syndrome (AIDS) and AIDS-related conditions; 8. Participation in any professional sports or hazardous sports, including racing of any kind on horse or wheels, mountaineering, rock climbing and scuba diving; 9. Any pre-existing condition (i.e. condition occurring before the payment of the initial payment). The above list of exclusions may differ from insurer to insurer. IMPORTANT CONCEPTS DEFINITIONS/EXPLANATIONS Events that will cause a Disability Income Insurance policy to be terminated
▪ Policy has reached its expiry date;
▪ Premiums due are not paid within the grace period;
▪ Insured dies;
▪ Insured reaches the expiry age as stated in the policy schedule;
▪ Insured resides outside Singapore for an aggregate period of more than a specified number of days (e.g. 300 days) within one policy year, without seeking the insurer’s prior written approval for continuation of cover; or
▪ Insured is not employed in a full-time occupation or profession for a continuous period of a specified number of days (except if he is disabled). Coverage and features of Group Disability Income Insurance Cover provided by a Group Disability Income Insurance policy works in the same way as an individual Disability Income Insurance policy. The benefits and exclusions are also the same General underwriting principles for both Individual and Group Disability Income Insurance The selection process in Group Disability Insurance policies is similar to that for the individual disability plans. As the group must have a Group Term Life Insurance policy before they are eligible for this disability policy, most of the underwriting criteria will have already been met. The additional factors that the underwriters will need to take into consideration are: 1. Nature of the company’s business; 2. Age of the individual employee; 3. Exact nature of work of each employee; 4. Salary of each employee; 5. Benefit period applied for; 6. Benefit amount applied for; and 7. Claims experience of the group under the previous Group Disability Income Insurance policy. General claims procedures for both Individual and Group Disability Income Insurance Claims procedures for individual or group disability income are similar.
▪ In the event of a claim, the insured must notify the insurer within a specified period of time (e.g. 60 days) from the date of commencement of the disability.
▪ Failure to comply with this requirement might cause the claim to be invalidated, unless the insured could prove that it was not possible for him to notify the insurer within the specified period, and that he had tried his best to do so.
▪ If the insured resides outside Singapore while the policy benefits are payable, the insurer may impose conditions. General claims documents for Disability Income Policy
▪ Claim form giving the personal particulars of the insured, as well as details of his occupation and disability;
▪ Claimant’s statement;
▪ Clinical Abstract form (see Appendix 4A);
▪ Physician’s statement/doctor’s statement to be completed by the insured’s attending physician giving full details of his disability;
▪ NRIC or birth certificate for verification of the insured’s age;
▪ Evidence of earnings, which can be in the form of income tax returns (IR8A form), copies of payslips for the 12 months prior to the commencement of disability, and letter from employer to certify that the claimant (insured) is not being paid during the period of disability;
▪ Letter from company certifying that the insured’s service has been terminated (if available);
▪ Copies of Medical Certificates;
▪ All available Laboratory and Tests results;
▪ A copy of the Police Report if the cause of the disability is due to an accident; and
▪ A copy of the Incident Report (required to be submitted to the Ministry of Manpower if the incident occurs at the workplace).
paragraphs.textJP: この保険証券には過剰保険を防止するための制限条項が含まれているため、請求が発生した場合に顧客が制限条項の適用を受けないよう、据置期間と付保すべき給与の割合が正しいことを確認する必要があります。

chapters.title: LONG-TERM CARE INSURANCE
outline: Other Benefits Provided Under LTC Insurance
learningOutcomes: explain Long-Term Care (LTC) Insurance
know the types of LTC Insurance
know the benefits offered under LTC Insurance
know the eligibility criteria for payment of LTC benefits
know what are considered as “Activities of Daily Living (ADLs)” and how they are related to the benefit payment under LTC Insurance
know the other benefits provided under LTC Insurance
explain maximum benefit sum payable and benefit payment term under LTC Insurance
know what is ElderShield
know the persons covered under ElderShield
know how to check ElderShield coverage
know the scope of protection under ElderShield and its premiums
explain when an insured is eligible to claim under ElderShield
know how to make a claim under ElderShield
know what is CareShield Life and how it provides better protection
know the differences between ElderShield and CareShield Life
explain the features of MediSave Care

paragraphs.text: Growing old gracefully and leading a healthy, independent lifestyle is the desire of most people. However, age-related illnesses and injuries may prevent them from fulfilling this desire. Chronic medical conditions like diabetes, stroke, hypertension, dementia, Alzheimer’s disease, and the complications that arise from these medical conditions may render the elderly people unable to care for themselves independently. They will need help with Activities of Daily Living (ADLs), such as feeding, bathing, dressing, using the toilet, and moving around. Some may even require special care in a facility, such as a nursing home. The costs of such care can strain one’s financial resources and, therefore, become a financial burden to one’s family.
paragraphs.textJP: 優雅に老いること、健康で自立した生活を送ることは、ほとんどの人の願いです。しかし、加齢に伴う疾病や傷害がこの願いの実現を妨げる場合があります。糖尿病、脳卒中、高血圧、認知症、アルツハイマー病などの慢性疾患、およびこれらの疾患から生じる合併症により、高齢者は自立して自己を介護することができなくなる場合があります。食事、入浴、着替え、排泄、移動などの日常生活動作（ADL）について援助が必要になります。中には施設での特別なケアを必要とする場合もあります。長期介護（LTC）保険は、自立して生活できなくなった者の介護費用を賄うための資金を提供するのに役立ちます。

paragraphs.text: Singapore’s population is rapidly ageing and by 2025, the nation is expected to attain “super-aged” status. One in four citizens will be aged 65 and above by 2030. An ageing population, coupled with increased longevity, will lead to higher national healthcare and long-term care expenditure, particularly for the elderly. With fewer children to support parents and grandparents, and the increasing numbers of elderly people living alone, more paid help, such as domestic workers, home-based caregivers and nurses, will also be needed. This will be a growing concern for the Government, as it will have to ensure that seniors have adequate protection against the cost of long-term care when such needs arise.
paragraphs.textJP: シンガポールの人口は急速に高齢化しており、2025年までに「超高齢」社会の地位に達すると予想されています。2030年までに市民の4人に1人が65歳以上になります。高齢化する人口と長寿の進展は、特に高齢者に対する国家の医療費と長期介護費の増大をもたらします。親や祖父母を支える子供が減少し、独居高齢者が増加するなか、家事労働者、在宅介護者、看護師などのより多くの有償の援助も必要になります。政府は少子高齢化に対処する施策の策定を迫られており、これは増大する懸念事項です。

paragraphs.text: In this chapter, we will look at how Long-Term Care Insurance can help provide funds to supplement the cost of financing an individual’s long-term care needs.
paragraphs.textJP: この章では、長期介護保険が個人の長期介護ニーズの資金調達費用を補助するための資金提供にどのように役立つかを見ていきます。

paragraphs.text: LTC Insurance is designed to meet the costs of care for a person who, because of an accident, illness, frailty or a combination of these, is unable to function independently. Instead, he must depend on others to help him with the most basic ADLs.
paragraphs.textJP: LTC保険は、事故、疾病、虚弱、またはこれらの組み合わせにより自立して機能することができない者の介護費用を賄うために設計されています。代わりに、最も基本的な日常生活動作（ADL）の支援を他者に頼らなければなりません。

paragraphs.text: There are two key advantages of buying LTC, while clients are still young and healthy, with no existing medical disabilities.  Firstly, the product will cost less as the premiums will rise considerably with age.  Secondly, buying at a younger age also means that there is less risk of being rejected for cover owing to existing disabilities or medical history.
paragraphs.textJP:
LTCを若くて健康で、既存の医学的障害がないうちに購入することには、2つの主要な利点があります。
▪ 第一に、保険料は年齢とともに大幅に上昇するため、商品のコストが低くなります。
▪ 第二に、若い年齢で購入することにより、保険者が申込を引き受ける可能性が高くなります。

paragraphs.text: LTC Insurance covers care generally excluded by other Medical Insurance. It will pay in addition to the insured’s other insurance policies e.g. MediShield Life, Hospital Income and Critical Illness Insurance policy.
paragraphs.textJP: LTC保険は、他の医療保険では一般に除外されるケアを補償します。MediShield Life、入院所得保険、重大疾病保険証券など、被保険者の他の保険証券に加えて支払われます。

paragraphs.text: Two types of LTC policies are available and they are: (a) “Service-based” policy pays a benefit when an insured needs to use the service which is covered under the policy (e.g. home care or nursing care). Benefits can be paid in two ways:  Reimbursement of the cost incurred in using one of the services covered under the policy, up to the covered amount; or  a pre-agreed insured amount regardless of the actual cost incurred for the services. (b) “Disability-based” policy pays a benefit when an insured:  Is diagnosed to be suffering from advanced dementia (including Alzheimer’s disease;  is assessed to be suffering significant limitations in ADLs; or  is evaluated to have a deterioration or loss of intellectual capacity resulting in a significant reduction in mental and social functioning, thus the need for continuous supervision.
paragraphs.textJP:
2種類のLTC保険証券が利用可能です：
(a) 「サービスベース」の保険証券は、被保険者が保険証券の下で補償されるサービス（例：在宅ケアまたは看護ケア）を利用する必要がある場合に給付を支払います。給付は2つの方法で支払われます：
▪ 保険証券の下で補償されるサービスの1つを利用した際に発生した費用を、補償額の上限まで償還
▪ 実際に発生した費用にかかわらず、事前に合意された保険金額を定額で支払い
(b) 「障害ベース」の保険証券は、被保険者が以下の場合に給付を支払います：
▪ 進行性認知症（アルツハイマー病を含む）と診断された場合
▪ 所定の最低数のADLを遂行できないと認定された場合

paragraphs.text: Benefits are paid monthly (a) to the insured upon being certified by an approved and registered medical practitioner to be unable to perform the specified minimum number of defined ADLs after a prescribed period. Some insurers pay 100% of the LTC monthly benefit if the insured is unable to perform at least the minimum number of ADLs, whereas others pay on a graduated scale. For example, if the insured cannot perform two of the six ADLs, only 50% of the LTC monthly benefit is payable, but if the insured cannot perform four out of the six ADLs, 100% of the LTC monthly benefit will then be payable.
paragraphs.textJP: 給付は、(a) 承認済みの登録医師により所定の最低数のADLを遂行することができないと証明された被保険者に対して、所定の期間後に月額で支払われます。一部の保険者は、被保険者が最低数のADLを遂行できない場合にLTC月額給付の100%を支払��ますが、他の保険者は段階的な比率で支払います。例えば、被保険者が6つのADLのうち2つを遂行できない場合はLTC月額給付の50%のみが支払われ、6つのADLのうち4つを遂行できない場合はLTC月額給付の100%が支払われます。

subsections.label: B
subsections.title: Eligibility Criteria For Payment Of LTC Benefits

paragraphs.text: The insured does not need not be hospitalised to be eligible for the LTC benefits. He can use the benefits received under his LTC to cover the costs of engaging a domestic helper (maid), staying in a nursing home, or to participating in an assisted-living facility or home-care programme.
paragraphs.textJP: 被保険者はLTC給付の適格性を得るために入院する必要はありません。LTCに基づいて受け取った給付を、家事労働者（メイド）の雇用費用、介護施設での滞在費用、または生活支援プログラムへの参加費用に充てることができます。

paragraphs.text: However, he must meet certain other criteria, before he can be eligible to claim for the LTC benefits.
paragraphs.textJP: ただし、LTC給付を請求する資格を得る前に、特定のその他の基準を満たす必要があります。

subsections.label: B1
subsections.title: Inability To Perform Activities Of Daily Living (ADLs) Or Advanced Dementia

paragraphs.text: Inability To Perform ADLs (a) One of the key criteria for paying the benefits in LTC policies is the insured’s inability to perform specific essential actions required for an individual to live independently without support. These essential actions are known as “Activities of Daily Living” (ADLs). (b) The definitions of these ADLs are important. Some policies pay the benefits for not being able to perform one out of six ADLs while others require three out of six ADLs to be affected for benefits . Premium for the former is more expensive than the latter. (c) The ADLs are defined as follows:  Washing: the ability to wash in the bath or shower (including getting into and out of the bath or shower) or wash by other means.  Dressing: the ability to put on, or take off, secure and unfasten all garments and, as appropriate, any braces, artificial limbs, or other surgical or medical appliances.  Feeding: the ability to feed oneself after the food has been prepared and made available.  Toileting: the ability to use the lavatory or manage bowel and bladder function through the use of protective undergarments (e.g. diapers) or surgical appliances (e.g. urinary catheters) if appropriate.  Mobility: the ability to move indoors from room to room on level surfaces.  Transferring: the ability to move from a bed to an upright chair or wheelchair, and vice versa. Washing Dressing Feeding Toileting Mobility Transferring (d) Failure to perform any of the above ADLs means that the insured will be unable to perform the task, even with the aid of assistive devices (e.g. wheelchair, clutches) on his own and always require the assistance of another person throughout the entire activity. Washing Dressing Feeding Mobility Toileting Transferring Activities of Daily Living (ADLs)
paragraphs.textJP:
ADLの遂行不能
(a) LTC保険証券で給付を支払うための主要な基準の1つは、被保険者が支援なしで自立して生活するために必要な特定の基本的動作を遂行できないことです。これらの基本的動作は日常生活動作（ADL）として知られています。
(b) これらのADLの定義は重要です。一部の保険証券は6つのADLのうち1つを遂行できない場合に給付を支払いますが、他の保険証券は6つのADLのうち3つが影響を受ける場合���給付を支払います。前者の保険料は後者より高くなります。
(c) ADLは以下のように定義されます：
▪ 入浴（Washing）— 入浴またはシャワーで体を洗う能力（浴槽やシャワーへの出入りを含む）、または他の手段で体を洗う能力
▪ 着替え（Dressing）— すべての衣服を着脱、留め、外す能力。適切な場合、装具、義肢、その他の外科的または医療的器具を含む
▪ 移動（Transferring）— ベッドから直立した椅子へ、またはその逆に移る能力
▪ 歩行（Mobility）— 平坦な面で室内を移動する能力
▪ 排泄（Toileting）— トイレまたは他の方法を使用して、膀胱および腸の機能を管理する能力
▪ 食事（Feeding）— 準備された食事を食べる能力

paragraphs.text: Advanced Dementia (a) Advanced dementia means a condition where the insured experiences a deterioration or loss of intellectual capacity or abnormal behaviour, arising from Alzheimer’s disease or irreversible organic disorders excluding neurosis and psychiatric illness. This condition results in significant reduction in mental and social functioning requiring the continual supervision of the insured to prevent the insured from harming others or himself. Advanced dementia is confirmed by clinical assessments accepted standardised questionnaires or tests. (b) The dementia must arise from an organic reason, and that the insured requires continual supervision.
paragraphs.textJP:
進行性認知症
(a) 進行性認知症とは、アルツハイマー病または不可逆的な器質性疾患に起因する知的能力の低下もしくは喪失、または異常行動を被保険者が経験する状態を意味します。神経症および精神疾患は除外されます。この状態は精神的および社会的機能の著しい低下をもたらし、被保険者が他者または自身を傷つけることを防ぐための継続的な監督が必要とされます。進行性認知症は、臨床評価、標準化された質問票または検査により確認されます。
(b) 認知症は器質的疾患から生じなければなりません。すなわち、脳の物理的変化に起因するものでなければなりません。

paragraphs.text: Besides the requirement of failure to perform the ADLs, the insured may also need to fulfil a deferred period requirement before being eligible to receive the LTC benefits. Most insurers have a deferred period of 90 days commencing from the first day of any continuous period of inability to perform the ADLs. Many seniors may suffer short periods of dependence after a stay in hospital for an illness or injury, and a deferred period will ensure that short-term claims are avoided and, therefore, reduce the premium.
paragraphs.textJP: ADLの遂行不能の要件に加えて、被保険者はLTC給付を受ける資格を得る前に、据置期間の要件も満たす必要がある場合があります。ほとんどの保険者は、ADLを遂行できない継続的な期間の初日から起算して90日の据置期間を設けています。多くの高齢者は疾病や傷害で入院した後、短期間の依存状態を経験する場合があり、据置期間を設けることで短期の請求が回避され、結果として保険料の軽減につながります。

paragraphs.text: LTC policy would not pay if the claim arose from a pre-existing condition that was not disclosed in the proposal form. If disclosed, the insurer may accept the application at standard premium rates, or at sub-standard premium rates, i.e. may charge a higher premium, accept the application with exclusions, or reject the application. The decisions taken regarding applications and the rates of premiums application are largely dependent on the severity of the conditions of the proposer (also called the applicant or the intending insured).
paragraphs.textJP: LTC保険証券は、申込書で開示されなかった既存の病態から請求が生じた場合、支払いを行いません。開示された場合、保険者は標準保険料率で申込を引き受けるか、標準外の保険料率（すなわち、より高い保険料を請求する）で引き受けるか、除外条項付きで申込を引き受けるか、または申込を拒否する場合があります。申込および保険料率に関する判断は、申込者（被保険者予定者）の状態の重症度に大きく依存します。

paragraphs.text: The purpose of the provision is to guard against people buying the coverage knowing that they have a condition that is likely to lead to a claim. This is also known as anti-selection against the insurer.
paragraphs.textJP: この規定の目的は、請求につながる可能性のある状態があることを知っていながら補償を購入する者を防ぐことです。これは保険者に対する逆選択（アンチセレクション）としても知られています。

paragraphs.text: The insurer may also impose other policy conditions for the insured to comply with, before they are eligible to claim the benefits.
paragraphs.textJP: 保険者はまた、被保険者が給付を請求する資格を得る前に遵守しなければならない他の保険証券条件を課す場合があります。

sections.title: Other Benefits Provided Under Ltc Insurance

paragraphs.text: Insurers also usually include a death benefit which is payable to the insured’s beneficiaries upon the death of the insured. However, the quantum is small, ranging from an indicative amount of S$5,000 to six times the LTC monthly benefit for example. Upon payment of the death benefit, the cover ceases. Deferred Period usually 90 Days
