You should also take note of the maximum aggregate sum assured that each client is entitled to if the insurer that you represent has such a practice. (e) Personal Accident Insurance For this class of policy, you need to find out the extent of its coverage, including the details of its exclusions and limitations. This will enable you to assess whether the prospective client is adequately covered. (f) Long-Term Care Insurance Review the benefits that are payable and the benefit trigger point, the definitions used for the Activities of Daily Living (ADLs) and the number of ADLs needed before the benefits are payable. In addition, you need to find out if it is a reimbursement type of benefit, where the payment is based on the actual amount of expenses incurred. (g) Hospital Cash Insurance Hospital Cash Insurance pays on top of all other Health Insurance plans. You need to find out the daily benefit and the maximum number of days that the benefit is payable. (h) Life Insurance & Employee Benefits Find out if the prospective client is covered under his company’s insurance and whether he has any Life Insurance policy. Payment from employee benefits will affect how the other policies (with Co-ordination of Benefit Clause) pay. The total and permanent disability benefit under the Life Insurance policy can be used to help the prospective client, to overcome his financial difficulties in the event that he is totally and permanently disabled.
paragraphs.textJP: 見込み客とその扶養家族が生命保険および健康保険に加入している場合、既存の保険証券の給付と現在のニーズを比較し、不足がないか確認する必要があります。

paragraphs.text: With the Fact-Find information, you can tell whether the prospective client has a need for Health Insurance and, if so, whether he can afford it. If the client does not have any need for more Health Insurance coverage, you should not persuade him to purchase any such policy. Instead, you should do a review with him when his circumstances change (e.g. having a newborn child). On the other hand, if the prospective client does have a need for Health Insurance, and he has the means to pay for it, the next step that you have to carry out is to quantify the prospective client’s Health Insurance needs.
paragraphs.textJP: ファクトファインドの情報により、見込み客が健康保険を必要としているかどうか、また必要な場合にそれを支払う余裕があるかどうかを判断できます。顧客がこれ以上の健康保険補償を必要としない場合、保険証券の購入を勧めるべきではありません。代わりに、状況が変化した場合（例：新生児の誕生）にレビューを行うべきです。一方、顧客に健康保険のニーズがあるが保険料を支払う余裕がない場合、許容可能な範囲で保険料を抑えたプランを推奨できます。

paragraphs.text: Needs quantification is not mandatory, but an industry “best practice”.
paragraphs.textJP: ニーズの定量化は義務ではありませんが、業界の「ベストプラクティス」です。

paragraphs.text: Disability Income Protection Needs is also known as Maintenance Costs. This is the amount that is required to meet the ongoing maintenance of the prospective client and his dependants, i.e. their daily living expenses.
paragraphs.textJP: 障害所得保護ニーズは維持費用（Maintenance Costs）としても知られています。これは、見込み客とその扶養家族の継続的な維持に必要な金額、すなわち日常の生活費です。

paragraphs.text: There are commonly three methods (as described below) to quantify this. Method 1 Using the monthly salary, 75% of it is his income protection need in the event of his disability. Disability Income Protection = 75% x monthly income : S$X Less Existing disability benefit per month : (S$X) Estimated level of disability income protection needed : S$XX Method 2 This method uses monthly expenses instead of income to determine the coverage needed. Total monthly expenses : S$X Less Existing disability benefit per month : (S$X) Estimated level of income protection needed : S$XX Monthly expenses should include the amount that the prospective client spends on himself, as well as on his family. This is because when a person is disabled, the usual expenses will continue, and on top of that, the prospective client also has to take care of his medical expenses. This method provides the minimum amount that is required to sustain the prospective client’s living expenses in the event of a disability. Example 14.2 shows you how to compute the amount of income protection needed in the event of a disability using Method 2. Example 14.2: Computing The Amount Of Disability Income Protection Needs Ben, aged 35 years, has indicated in his Fact-Find Document that his family’s living expenses (inclusive of that of his own) is S$3,000 per month. He has also indicated that he wishes to have an income in the event that he is disabled and is not able to work. So far, he does not have any insurance coverage other than the Central Provident Fund (CPF) Board’s Dependants’ Protection Scheme (DPS)1, Home Protection Scheme and IncomeShield Plan MA, as well as his employer’s Group Term Life Insurance of S$100,000 sum assured, and Hospital and Surgical Insurance coverage of up to S$50,000 without any sub-limits. The amount of disability income protection that he needs is: Total monthly expenses : S$3,000 Less Existing disability benefit per month : S$ 0 Existing hospital cash benefit per month : S$ 0 ________ Estimated level of income protection needed : S$3,000 Method 3 There may be circumstances under which the prospective client may not want, is not able to afford a Disability Income Insurance (DII) policy or simply does not have an income to be eligible for a DII policy. For such a prospective client, you can help to provide him with total and permanent disability benefits usually incorporated into a Life Insurance policy. This method provides a lump sum benefit should a person become totally and permanently disabled. Example 14.3 illustrates how you can compute the amount of total and permanent disability benefits which the prospective client will need. Example 14.3: Computing The Amount Of Total And Permanent Disability (TPD) Benefit Following Example 14.2, the amount of TPD benefit that Ben needs per year is as follows: Yearly income needed = family’s average living expenses x 12 = S$3,000 x 12 = S$36,000 1 DPS is an affordable term life insurance that provides insured members and their families with some money should the insured members pass away, suffer from Terminal Illness or Total Permanent Disability. It covers them for a benefit of S$70,000 up to 60 years old. From age 60 age to age 65 years, the coverage is S$55,000. Assuming that the annual inflation rate is 2% and expected rate of return from investments remains constant at 4%, the expected rate of return from funds (adjusted for inflation) = 4% - 2% =2% If the number of years that the income is needed is 25 (to be determined by the client), then the estimated funds required to provide income is: =S$36,000 x 19.9139 (refer to Table A2 at the end of this Study Text) =S$716,900 Less Funds available + Existing Insurance* = S$(46,000 + 100,000) = S$146,000 Additional amount of total and permanent disability benefit required = S$(716,900 – 146,000) = S$570,900 *Only consider those existing insurance policies which have a TPD benefit. Group Term Life and DPS both have TPD benefits.
paragraphs.textJP:
これを定量化する一般的な3つの方法があります。

方法1：月給を使用し、その75%を障害時の所得保護ニーズとします。
障害所得保護 = 75% × 月収：S$X
控除：既存の障害給付（月額）：(S$X)
必要な障害所得保護の推定額：S$XX

方法2：月収の代わりに月間支出を使用します。
障害所得保護 = 月間支出合計：S$X
控除：既存の障害給付（月額）：(S$X)
必要な障害所得保護の推定額：S$XX

方法3：将来価値法を使用します。障害期間中に必要な総額を現在価値に割り引いて計算します。

例：Eugeneの場合
月収：S$4,800
月間支出：S$3,600

方法1：75% × S$4,800 = S$3,600（月額ニーズ）、既存の障害給付なし → S$3,600/月の障害所得保護が必要
方法2：月間支出S$3,600、既存の障害給付なし → S$3,600/月が必要
方法3：将来の支出総額を年金終価係数で計算

Eugeneには以下の保険商品をそれぞれ検討する必要があります：
1. 医療費保険（Medical Expense Insurance）
2. 障害所得保険（Disability Income Insurance）
3. 入院日額保険（Hospital Cash Insurance）
4. 重大疾病保険（Critical Illness Insurance）
5. 長期介護保険（Long-Term Care Insurance）

paragraphs.text: Having learnt how to determine the maintenance cost, let us now learn how to determine the medical costs.
paragraphs.textJP: 維持費用の決定方法を学んだので、次に医療費用の決定方法を学びましょう。

paragraphs.text: Medical costs refer to the immediate hospital and medical expenses associated with an injury or illness resulting in the prospective client being hospitalized for treatment or surgery.
paragraphs.textJP: 医療費用とは、見込み客が入院する原因となった傷害や疾病に関連する即時の入院・医療費を指します。

paragraphs.text: To quantify medical cost needs, it is important to discuss these with the prospective client: (a) The hospital type and ward the client would opt for in the event of hospitalisation; (b) his premium affordability to insure part of the compulsory co-payments or if he can accept to self-insure the co-payments; and (c) The continuous affordability of medical insurance premiums when he is no longer working.
paragraphs.textJP:
医療費のニーズを定量化するために、見込み客と以下について話し合うことが重要です：
(a) 入院時に顧客が選択する病院の種類と病棟
(b) 強制的な共同保険の一部を付保するための保険料の支払い余裕があるか、または共同保険を自己保険で受け入れられるか
(c) 就労しなくなった後の医療保険の保険料の継続的な支払い余裕

paragraphs.text: It could also help for the client to know the costs of specific medical procedures. Data on fee benchmarks are available on the Ministry of Health (MOH) Website. In addition, highlight and remind the prospective client that his Group Insurance (if any) is not portable, and he may lose his Group Insurance coverage when he leaves the organisation later.
paragraphs.textJP: 特定の医療処置の費用を顧客が知ることも有益です。料金ベンチマークのデータは保健省（MOH）のウェブサイトで入手できます。さらに、見込み客に対して、団体保険（加入している場合）はポータブルではなく、後日組織を離れると団体保険の補償を失う可能性があることを強調し注意喚起してください。

paragraphs.text: The way to compute the amount of Hospital Cash Insurance required is as follows: Total monthly expenses S$ X Less Existing hospital cash benefit per month (S$ X) Estimated level of income protection needed S$XX Assuming Eugene’s monthly expenses is S$3,600; the amount of Hospital Cash Income required is as follows: Monthly expenses S$ 3,600 Less Existing hospital cash benefit per month (S$ 0) Estimated level of income protection needed S$ 3,600 Therefore, the amount of Hospital Cash income required is: = S$$3,600÷ 30 days = S$120 per day
paragraphs.textJP:
必要な入院日額保険の金額の計算方法は以下のとおりです：
月間総支出：S$X
控除：既存の入院日額給付（月額）：(S$X)
必要な所得保護の推定額：S$XX

Eugeneの場合、月間支出がS$3,600とすると：
月間支出：S$3,600
控除：既存の入院日額給付（月額）：(S$0)
必要な所得保護の推定額：S$3,600

したがって、Eugeneの日額入院給付額 = S$3,600 ÷ 30 = S$120/日

paragraphs.text: Besides taking care of hospitalisation expenses and disability income, the prospective client may also want a lump sum payment for his rehabilitation expenses, to defray expenses not covered by other Health Insurance policies, and to maintain his family living expenses, as well as to handle any emergency expenses, if he contracts a dread disease or critical illness. A Critical Illness Insurance policy is suitable for these purposes. You will need to calculate the amount of Critical Illness Insurance cover that is required.
paragraphs.textJP: 入院費用と障害所得の補償に加えて、見込み客は重大疾病に罹患した場合のリハビリテーション費用、他の医療保険証券でカバーされない支出の補填、家族の生活費の維持、および緊急費用に対処するための一時金の支払いを希望する場合もあります。重大疾病保険証券は、この保護を見込み客に提供できます。

paragraphs.text: The calculation is really more of an art than a science, since there are many uncertainties. Some factors to consider for the prospective client are described below: (a) Which dread disease is he most susceptible to? His family medical history may shed some light on this answer. Therefore, medical costs can be approximated. (b) His family’s needs and expenses. (c) His family’s level of dependence on him. (d) Does he already have sufficient Medical Expense Insurance and/or Disability Income Insurance? (e) His desired level of medical treatment, e.g. private or public hospital. (f) Whether he wants to buy an Additional Benefit Type or Acceleration Benefit Type of CI Insurance. If he decides on the Additional Benefit Type, then he must be informed that the coverage will normally cease at the age of 65 years, unlike the Acceleration Benefit Type. Since the Acceleration Benefit Type is usually attached to a Whole Life Insurance policy, this type of benefit will last the whole of life. (g) Does he want a benefit on early diagnosis of a critical illness, the ability to make multiple critical illness claims, or coverage for other conditions such as diabetes or mental illness? (h) His preference and comfort level. Some prospective clients will require only S$100,000 CI Insurance cover, while others will feel that even a S$500,000 cover is insufficient.
paragraphs.textJP:
計算は多くの不確実性があるため、科学よりも技術に近いです。見込み客にとって考慮すべき要因には以下が含まれます：
(a) 最もかかりやすい重大疾病は何か？家族の病歴がヒントになる場合があります。これにより医療費を概算できます
(b) 家族のニーズと支出
(c) 本人への家族の依存度
(d) 利用可能な財務資源（貯蓄、投資等）
(e) 重大疾病の治療、リハビリテーション、日常生活への復帰に必要な期間
(f) 現在の団体保険による補償（ポータブルでないことに注意）

例：Eugeneが癌に罹患した場合を想定
以下の費用を見積もります：
・入院および手術費用
・放射線/化学療法の費用
・投薬費用
・リハビリテーション費用
・休職期間中の家族の生活費

合計から既存のCI補償を差し引き、不足額を算出します。

Eugeneの場合：
推定必要CI保険金額：S$200,000
既存のCI補償：S$0
不足額：S$200,000

paragraphs.text: After an agreement has been reached with him as to the CI Insurance cover required, you will be able to calculate the shortfall in cover by deducting away the current CI Insurance cover that he already has.
paragraphs.textJP: 必要なCI保険補償について見込み客と合意に達した後、既存のCI保険補償を差し引くことにより補償の不足額を計算できます。

paragraphs.text: Having seen how a prospective client’s needs for Health Insurance are quantified, we now move on to see how to go about recommending the most suitable products to meet the prospective client’s needs.
paragraphs.textJP: 見込み客の健康保険ニーズがどのように定量化されるかを見たので、次に最も適切な商品を推奨する方法に進みます。

paragraphs.text: There are three basic principles that you should be aware of before making any product recommendations to the prospective client. They are: (a) recommend products only if he needs them; (b) recommend products which are only the most suitable for him, given his existing circumstances; and (c) if he needs a product that the company you represent does not carry, let him know, so that he can find alternatives.
paragraphs.textJP: 見込み顧客に商品を推奨する前に知っておくべき基本原則が3つあります。（a）見込み顧客が必要としている場合にのみ商品を推奨する、（b）見込み顧客の現在の状況を踏まえて最も適切な商品のみを推奨する、（c）見込み顧客が代理している会社が取り扱っていない商品を必要としている場合には、代替品を探せるよう見込み顧客に告知する、の3点です。

paragraphs.text: In order for the insurance representative to determine if a product is suitable for the prospective client, he needs to have a good understanding of the available products.
paragraphs.textJP: 保険担当者が商品が見込み客に適切かどうかを判断するためには、保険者が提供する商品を十分に理解している必要があります。

paragraphs.text: The insurance representative should be familiar with the following aspects of your company’s products: (a) What is the product range? (b) What does the product serve to do? For example, reimbursement of expenses or replacement of income? (c) What are the eligibility criteria for making a claim under the policy? (d) Does the policy have any waiting period? If so, find out the duration and effective date of the waiting period. (e) Is the policy guaranteed renewable, optionally renewable, conditionally renewable or cancellable? (f) What are the exclusions and limitations under the policy? (g) Does the policy have any special features, such as the survival period under a Critical Illness Insurance policy, and the requirement to inform the insurer of any change in occupation under a Disability Income Insurance policy? (h) Is the policy subject to any Co-ordination of Benefit Clause? (i) Does the policy have any deductible or co-insurance feature? (j) Is there any per day, per year or per lifetime limit under the policy? How much are the limits? (k) When and how are the benefits payable? For example, a Hospital Cash (Income) policy pays a flat amount for each day of hospitalisation capped at a certain number of days. (l) What is the duration of cover under the policy? (m) What are the premiums and the frequency modes for premium payments, and are they subject to changes? (n) Is there a grace period for the payment of premium and, if so, what is the time frame? (o) What is the implication of non-payment of premium? (p) What are the medical and non-medical limits (i.e. the underwriting guidelines on when a medical test is required for the assessment of an application) of the products? (q) Which types of policies have non-forfeiture options? (r) Is there any geographical limit imposed on the policy? (s) Is the policy available in multiple currencies for claim payment (e.g. in case of overseas medical treatment benefit)?
paragraphs.textJP:
保険担当者は、自社商品の以下の側面に精通しているべきです：
(a) 商品の範囲は何か？
(b) 商品は何を目的としているか？例えば、費用の償還か所得の代替か？
(c) 保険証券に基づく請求の適格基準は何か？
(d) 保険証券に待機期間はあるか？ある場合、期間と有効日はいつか？
(e) 保険証券の給付は何か？また、共同負担はあるか？
(f) 保険証券の免責事項は何か？
(g) 保険証券のその他の特徴（例：ライダー、ボーナス等）は何か？
(h) 保険証券の保険料はいくらか？
(i) 保険料の支払い方法（例：現金、MediSave、またはその組み合わせ）は？
(j) 保険証券の請求手続きは何か？
(k) 保険証券のその他の条件（例：更新可能性、解約条件等）は何か？

上記の知識に加えて、競合他社の類似商品との比較も可能にしておくべきです。これにより、見込み客に対して自社商品の優位性を説明したり、客観的な比較を提示したりできます。

paragraphs.text: Affordability is important when selecting the most suitable product to recommend to the prospective client. Most policies require regular premiums to be paid. Being able to afford the initial premium does not mean that he will be able to sustain the regular payments. For policies paid with Medisave, ensure that his Medisave is enough to support continuity of the policy, either with his current balance or his future regular contributions to his Medisave Account. A prospective client who cannot afford the premiums in the longer term will allow his policy to lapse. The insurer, the insurance representative and the prospective client do not benefit from early policy lapses. From the prospective client’s standpoint, he loses the coverage. The insurance representative loses the prospective client’s goodwill and the commission payable under the policy. The insurer has to absorb the high initial cost of underwriting the policy.
paragraphs.textJP: 手頃さは、見込み客に推奨する最も適切な商品を選択する際に重要です。ほとんどの保険証券は、保険証券を有効に維持するために定期的な保険料の支払いを必要とします。初回の保険料を支払えることは、定期的な支払いを持続できることを意味しません。MediSaveで支払う保険証券の場合、現在の残高またはCPFへの継続的な拠出によりMediSaveが保険証券の継続を支えるのに十分であることを確認してください。保険担当者は、被保険者が保険料を支払えなくなるリスクに注意すべきです。特に退職後は収入が減少するため、保険料を負担できなくなり保険証券が失効する可能性があります。

subsections.label: C
subsections.title: Important Points To Note On Policy Selection

paragraphs.text:
When deciding on which policy is most suitable for the prospective client, you will have to consider all the factors which we have gone through in this chapter. In addition, it is important that you should also consider the points as listed in Table 14.1 below. Table 14.1: Important Points To Note On Policy Selection
▪ Ensure that the new recommended policy provides cover only for his coverage shortfall to minimise over-insurance.
▪ Ensure that the prospective client has the longest coverage possible, as Health Insurance is needed most when one is old.
▪ Ensure that the prospective client can afford the premium in the long term.
▪ Ensure that the prospective client is not over-insured.
▪ Ensure that the policy insures the right life (e.g. if the husband is the sole breadwinner, then you should ensure that he is properly covered first, before you recommend any products to his dependants).
▪ Ensure that the prospective client with dependants has sufficient death protection in place.
▪ Recommend a rider if it can help to save premiums (provided that the expiry age of the rider will not be shortened by the term of the basic policy).
▪ For Critical Illness (CI) Insurance, take note of the two types of cover - Additional Benefit Type and Acceleration Benefit Type. Discuss the following with the client - expiry age, premium difference, whether he should take up a CI rider if he has existing life policies.
▪ Consider attaching the CI Waiver of Premium rider for the client who has other life insurance protection.
▪ For the prospective client who is covered under his employer-sponsored Medical Expense Insurance (MEI) and has a need for additional MEI cover, ensure that the proposed plan complements the one provided by the employer. For example, most employer-sponsored MEI policies provide cover from the first dollar onwards, but the coverage is low. In such a case, you can propose a Major MEI to your client.
▪ Always check for alternative policies to the one that you are recommending offering the prospective client more choices. This gives the prospective client more flexibility in choosing the premiums and benefits that meet his needs.
paragraphs.textJP:
見込み客に最も適切な保険証券を決定する際、この章で検討したすべての要因を考慮する必要があります。さらに、以下の表14.1に記載された点も考慮することが重要です。

表14.1：保険証券選択時の重要な注意点
▪ 推奨する新しい保険証券は、補償の不足部分のみをカバーすることを確認する（過剰な補償を推奨しない）
▪ 補償の重複を避ける（例：2つの保険証券が同じリスクをカバーしていないか確認）
▪ 見込み客の予算（保険料の支払い余裕）に適合することを確認する
▪ 保険証券の待機期間を説明する
▪ 免責事項と制限事項を明確に説明する
▪ 既存の保険証券の乗換えを推奨する場合、新しい保険証券が既存のものよりも顧客に有利であることを確認する
▪ 乗換えの際は、新しい保険証券の待機期間、既存の病態の除外、保険料の変更等のリスクを説明する
▪ 推奨のプロセスと理由を文書化する
▪ 顧客が十分な情報に基づいた意思決定を行えるよう支援する
▪ 各保険証券の主要な特徴、給付、免責事項、保険料を比較するための要約資料を提供する

paragraphs.text: To ensure that the prospective client understands the products recommended and the reasons for your recommendations, you should explain the features and benefits of the recommended products, and how these fit into his situation.
paragraphs.textJP: 見込み顧客が推奨された商品と推奨理由を理解できるよう、推奨商品の特徴・給付内容と、それが見込み顧客の状況にどのように合致するかを説明する必要があります。

paragraphs.text: Before meeting the prospective client to go through your recommendation, plan how you would like to present to him. It is advisable that you follow a systematic process.
paragraphs.textJP: 見込み顧客への推奨内容を説明するための面談前に、提示方法を計画しておくことをお勧めします。体系的なプロセスに従うことが望ましいです。

paragraphs.text: An example below shows a systematic and sequential process to explain your recommendations to the prospective client. 1. State the purpose of the product (i.e. the need which is actually being met by this product). 2. Give a description of the nature of the product. 3. Brief the prospective client on the benefits and limitations of the product. 4. Give a detailed explanation of the options within the product. 5. Give a summary of the reasons why the product is being recommended. 6. Explain the benefit illustration and highlight the guaranteed and nonguaranteed benefits (if applicable). 7. Disclose any distribution costs, charges and expenses under the policy.
paragraphs.textJP:
以下に、見込み顧客への推奨内容を説明するための体系的かつ順序立てたプロセスの例を示します。
1. 商品の目的（この商品によって実際にどのニーズが満たされるか）を説明する。
2. 商品の性質について説明する。
3. 商品の給付内容と制限事項を説明する。
4. 商品内のオプションについて詳しく説明する。
5. 商品を推奨する理由の概要を説明する。
6. 給付内容のイラストを説明し、確定給付と不確定給付（該当する場合）を強調する。
7. 証券に係る販売コスト、手数料および費用を開示する。

paragraphs.text: During the meeting, communicate clearly and simply. Avoid using technical jargons. If the prospective client accepts your recommendations, you have to ensure that all relevant forms required by your principal (insurer) are properly completed. In the case where the prospective client decides not to take up any of your recommendations, you may seek to clarify his reasons for not accepting your recommendations. However, you should not insist if the prospective client refuses to explain. Thank the prospective client for his time before leaving.
paragraphs.textJP: 面談中は、明確かつ平易に伝えてください。専門用語の使用は避けてください。見込み顧客が推奨内容を受け入れた場合は、保険者（保険会社）が必要とするすべての関連書類が適切に記入されていることを確認する必要があります。見込み顧客が推奨内容を受け入れない場合は、受け入れない理由を確認することができます。ただし、見込み顧客が説明を断った場合は、強要してはなりません。その場合は見込み顧客の時間に感謝して退席してください。

paragraphs.text: The process of identifying and satisfying your client’s needs does not stop with the implementation of the initial recommendations. Your client’s personal circumstances are likely to change and new needs may surface over time. Such changes may affect the initial product recommendations, as they may no longer be adequate. Regular reviews will ensure that your existing client continues to receive quality service from you, and also reinforces your relationship with your existing client.
paragraphs.textJP: 顧客のニーズを特定し満足させるプロセスは、最初の推奨内容の実施をもって終わるものではありません。顧客の個人的な状況は変化することが多く、時間の経過とともに新たなニーズが生じることもあります。このような変化は、当初の商品推奨が適切でなくなる可能性があるため影響を与えることがあります。定期的な見直しにより、既存顧客が継続して質の高いサービスを受けられるようにするとともに、顧客との関係をさらに強化することができます。

paragraphs.text: The question of when and how often the existing client’s position should be reviewed depends, to some extent, on the initial advice provided by you, and also on the existing client’s own wishes for a review. Generally, you should do a review with your existing client under the following circumstances: (a) a change in the client’s personal circumstances, such as the birth of a child; (b) external developments, such as changes in the CPF ruling which may have an impact on the client’s financial position and/or the appropriateness of products already held; (c) the original products purchased by the client are not adequate to cover all his needs; and (d) the launching of new products in the financial services market.
paragraphs.textJP: 既存顧客のポジションをいつ、どのくらいの頻度で見直すかは、当初に提供したアドバイスの内容や、既存顧客自身の見直し希望にある程度依存します。一般的には、以下のような状況で既存顧客との見直しを実施する必要があります。（a）子供の誕生など、顧客の個人的な状況の変化、（b）顧客の財務状況や保有する商品の適切性に影響を及ぼす可能性のあるCPF制度の変更など外部環境の変化、（c）顧客が当初購入した商品がすべてのニーズを満たすのに十分でなくなった場合、（d）金融サービス市場での新商品の発売。

paragraphs.text: Regular reviews benefit both you and your client. As a professional insurance representative, you should ensure that the products recommended meet with the client’s current needs. You should also seek to arrange regular meetings with him to address his future needs.
paragraphs.textJP: 定期的な見直しは、あなたと顧客双方に利益をもたらします。プロの保険外交員として、推奨した商品が顧客の現在のニーズを満たしていることを確認する必要があります。また、将来のニーズに対応するため、定期的な面談の設定を心がけてください。

paragraphs.text:
The long-term needs of the client should be a principal concern, and your relationship with him should also be an ongoing one. Do not recommend any further Health Insurance policies to the prospective client if he has adequate medical cover, as he may not be able to claim under all the insurance policies. FINANCIAL NEEDS ANALYSIS CHAPTER IMPORTANT CONCEPTS DEFINITIONS/EXPLANATIONS Needs selling versus product selling Needs selling is more desirable than product selling because it is:
▪ Service-oriented.
▪ Not pressure selling.
▪ Relationship based. What is fact-finding? • Fact-finding is the process of obtaining answers to a series of questions about a prospective client’s personal circumstances, finance and ambitions, etc. for the future. Purposes served by the various sections of the Fact-Find Document
▪ Important notice to client » Gives the prospective client information on who the insurance representative is, which insurers he represents and what product classes he can provide advice on. » Highlights to the prospective client the importance of providing accurate information on the Fact-Find Document.
▪ Application type » Types of disclosure. » Application type 1,2 or 3.
▪ Personal information » Essential for administrative purposes on the part of the company. » Preliminary assessment of the types of products that will likely be needed. » Information pertaining to age is essential for underwriting and premium determination.
▪ Information on employment details » Eligibility for Disability Income Insurance (DII). » Determine the deferred period for a DII policy. » Know whether the prospective client is exposed to any occupational hazards. » Affordability.
▪ Information on details of spouse and dependants » Identification in the event of a claim; » Premium determination; » Any occupational hazards (in respect of the spouse); » Eligibility to be covered as a dependant (this is because A&H Insurance policies that provide coverage for any dependant has an age limit to qualify as a dependant); and » Determine whether there is a need to provide A&H Insurance cover for the dependants.
▪ Existing health insurance policies / existing insurance portfolio » Indicate how far the prospective clients’ existing plan(s) will go towards meeting their needs in the event of ill health or disability.
▪ Personal Priorities and Objectives » Enable the representative to tell what the prospective client’s priorities are with regard to the type of A&H policies that he may need. IMPORTANT CONCEPTS DEFINITIONS/EXPLANATIONS Purposes served by the various sections of the Fact-Find Document
▪ Health condition » Help to determine whether the prospective client is insurable and, if so, on what terms.
▪ Medical Insurance for Migrant Workers » If the prospective client is purchasing the plan for a migrant worker holding a S-pass/ Work Permit, find out if they are intending to comply with Ministry of Manpower’s enhanced Medical Insurance requirement.
▪ Replacement of policy » If prospective client’s answer is “yes”, the representative is required to explain to the prospective client the consequences of replacing policies.
▪ Customer’s financials » Ascertain the affordability of the recommendation and plans, and facilitates the planning of financial needs.
▪ Advisor's declaration » Declaration on treating the information given in the Fact-Find Document with strict confidentiality and use it only for the purpose of determining suitable insurance products, and not for any other purposes, bearing in mind the requirements under the Personal Data Protection Act 2012 » Establish trust between the representative and the prospective client. Sample questions to be taken into consideration when going through the prospective client’s Fact- Find Document to identify his needs
▪ Does he have an emergency fund in place?
▪ What is his employment status and occupation?
▪ Which life stage is he in?
▪ Does he have any dependants?
▪ Does he or his dependants (if any) have any Individual Life or Health Insurance, or Group Medical Insurance (such as Group Critical Illness Insurance, Group Hospital & Surgical Insurance) in place?
▪ Does he or his dependants have a need for Life or Health Insurance?
▪ Can he afford to purchase any insurance policy that you are going to propose to him?
▪ Has he stated his priorities correctly? Importance of an emergency fund Guard against the breadwinner’s loss of a job or a short-term disability that interrupts the financial flow of income to the family. Which stage of the prospective client’s life cycle versus where the need to protect one’s earnings against disability and ill health, is critical
▪ During active employment years, it is crucial to ensure that one protects his earnings against disability resulting from injury or long-term sickness.
▪ Need for protection against ill health applies to everyone at any stage in his life.
▪ For older people, they will have a greater need for Critical Illness Insurance, Medical Expense Insurance and Long-Term Care Insurance, as there is a higher chance of them falling sick or getting injured.
▪ If there are dependants, provision should be made for their medical expenses to avoid any financial burden, in case any of the dependants contracts a major illness or becomes disabled. Know what to do if the prospective client has a:
▪ Disability Income Insurance
▪ Compare the level of cover with his current income level to see if the cover is sufficient.
▪ Check if the policy covers up to the target retirement age, the length of the deferred period, and whether the prospective client’s employer provides him with any disability income benefit.
▪ Remind the prospective client not to over-insure because of the Limitation Clause under the Disability Income Insurance policy. Know what to do if the prospective client has a:
▪ Life Insurance with Total & Permanent Disability Benefit Check for any TPD benefits that the prospective client already has by reviewing the existing life insurance policies. IMPORTANT CONCEPTS DEFINITIONS/EXPLANATIONS Know what to do if the prospective client has a:
▪ Medical Expense Insurance
▪ Find out the exact coverage, such as the hospital and ward entitlement of the plan, the annual and lifetime limits, and the policy co-insurance and deductible, if any.
▪ Look at the expiry age and the renewability condition.
▪ Ensure that the prospective client will be adequately covered in his preferred hospital and ward type in the event of hospitalisation, and that he will enjoy the coverage for as long as possible and will not risk losing his coverage at a time when he needs it most.
▪ Keep in mind the Co-ordination of Benefit Clause. Know what to do if the prospective client has a:
▪ Critical Illness Insurance
▪ Review whether it is a stand-alone policy or a rider attached to an ILP, Term, Endowment or Whole Life Insurance policy.
▪ Take note of the maximum aggregate sum assured that each client is entitled to, if the insurer has such a practice. Know what to do if the prospective client has a:
▪ Personal Accident Insurance Find out the extent of its coverage, including the details of its exclusions and limitations to assess whether the prospective client is adequately covered. Know what to do if the prospective client has a::
▪ Long-Term Care Insurance
▪ Review the benefits that are payable and the benefit trigger point, the definitions used for the Activities of Daily Living (ADLs) and the number of ADLs needed before the benefits are payable.
▪ Find out if it is a reimbursement type of benefit, where the payment is based on the actual amount of expenses incurred. Know what to do if the prospective client has a:
▪ Hospital Cash Insurance Find out the daily benefit and the maximum number of days that the benefit is payable. Know what to do if the prospective client has a:
▪ Life Insurance & Employee Benefits Find out if the prospective client is covered under his company’s insurance and whether he has any Life Insurance policy. Quantify disability income protection needs
▪ Method 1 » Using the monthly salary, 75% of it is his income protection need in the event of his disability.
▪ Method 2 » This method uses monthly expenses instead of income to determine the coverage needed.
▪ Method 3 » This method provides a lump sum benefit should a person become totally and permanently disabled. Quantify medical and ancillary costs To quantify medical cost needs, the representative needs to discuss and make some assumptions with the prospective clients as follows:
▪ The most common conditions for a person to be hospitalised;
▪ The cost of treatment for any one particular illness, such as a heart condition;
▪ The hospital type and ward the client would opt for in the event of hospitalisation;
▪ The need to provide coverage for medical cost from the first dollar or can he accept a co-payment;
▪ The continuous affordability of medical insurance premiums when he is no longer working. Quantify hospital cash income needs Estimated level of protection needed = Total monthly expense Less Existing hospital cash benefit per month IMPORTANT CONCEPTS DEFINITIONS/EXPLANATIONS Quantify Critical Illness Insurance Calculation is really more of an art than a science, since there are many uncertainties. Some factors to consider for the prospective client are described below:
▪ Which dread disease is he most susceptible to? His family medical history may shed some light on this answer. Therefore, medical costs can be approximated.
▪ His family’s needs and expenses.
▪ His family’s level of dependence on him.
▪ Does he already have sufficient Medical Expense Insurance and/or Disability Income Insurance?
▪ His desired level of medical treatment, e.g. private or public hospital.
▪ Whether he wants to buy an Additional Benefit Type or Acceleration Benefit Type of CI Insurance. If he decides on the Additional Benefit Type, then he must be informed that the coverage will normally cease at the age of 65 years, unlike the Acceleration Benefit Type. Since the Acceleration Benefit Type is usually attached to a Whole Life Insurance policy, this type of benefit will last the whole of life.
▪ Does he want a benefit on early diagnosis of a critical illness, does he want to be able to make multiple critical illness claims, does he want to cover other conditions such as diabetes or mental illness?
▪ His preference and comfort level. Some prospective clients will require only S$50,000 CI Insurance cover, while others will feel that even S$500,000 cover is insufficient. Basic principles before making any product recommendation
▪ Recommend products only if the prospective client needs them.
▪ Recommend products which are only the most suitable for the prospective client, given his existing circumstances.
▪ If prospective client needs a product that the company you represent does not carry, let him know, so that he can find alternatives. Sample questions to ask for better understanding of the product
▪ What is the product range?
▪ What does the product serve to do? For example, reimbursement of expenses or replacement of income?
▪ What are the eligibility criteria for making a claim under the policy?
▪ Does the policy have any waiting period? If so, find out the duration and effective date of the waiting period.
▪ Is the policy guaranteed renewable, optionally renewable, conditionally renewable or cancellable?
▪ What are the exclusions and limitations under the policy?
▪ Does the policy have any special features, such as the survival period under a Critical Illness Insurance policy, and the requirement to inform the insurer of any change in occupation under a Disability Income Insurance policy?
▪ Is the policy subject to any Co-ordination of Benefit Clause?
▪ Does the policy have any deductible or co-insurance feature?
▪ Is there any per day, per year or per lifetime limit under the policy? How much are the limits?
▪ When and how are the benefits payable? For example, a Hospital Cash (Income) policy pays a flat amount for each day of hospitalisation capped at a certain number of days.
▪ What are the distribution costs, charges and expenses levied on the products?
▪ What is the duration of cover under the policy?
▪ What are the premiums and the frequency modes for premium payments, and are they subject to changes?
▪ Is there a grace period allowed for the payment of premium due and, if so, what is the time frame?
▪ What is the implication of non-payment of premium?
▪ What are the medical and non-medical limits (i.e. the underwriting guidelines on when a medical test is required for the assessment of an application) of the products?
▪ Which types of policies have non-forfeiture options?
▪ Is there any geographical limit imposed on the policy? IMPORTANT CONCEPTS DEFINITIONS/EXPLANATIONS Why is affordability an important factor when selecting a suitable product to recommend to the prospective client?
▪ Affordability is an important factor when selecting the most suitable product to recommend to the prospective client.
▪ Most policies require regular premiums to be paid. Being able to afford the initial premium does not mean that he will be able to sustain the regular payments.
▪ The insurer, the insurance representative and the prospective client do not benefit from early policy lapses. » The prospective client loses the coverage. » The insurance representative loses the prospective client’s goodwill and the commission payable under the policy. » The insurer has to absorb the high initial cost of underwriting the policy. Important points on policy selection
▪ Ensure that the new recommended policy provides cover only for his coverage shortfall to minimise over-insurance.
▪ Ensure that the prospective client has the longest coverage possible.
▪ Ensure that the prospective client can afford the premium in the long term.
▪ Ensure that the prospective client is not over-insured.
▪ Ensure that the policy insures the right life.
▪ Ensure that the prospective client with dependants has sufficient death protection in place.
▪ Recommend a rider if it can help to save premiums.
▪ For Critical Illness (CI) Insurance, take note of the two types of cover - Additional Benefit Type and Acceleration Benefit Type.
▪ Discuss the following with the client - expiry age, premium difference, whether he should take up a CI rider if he has existing life policies.
▪ Consider to attach the CI Waiver of Premium rider for the client who has other life insurance protection.
▪ For the prospective client who is covered under his employer-sponsored Medical Expense Insurance (MEI) and has a need for additional MEI cover, ensure that the proposed plan complement the one provided by the employer.
▪ Always check for alternative policies to the one that you are recommending. Example of a systematic process of explaining recommendations to the prospective client
▪ State the purpose of the product (i.e. the need which is actually being met by this product).
▪ Give a description of the nature of the product.
▪ Brief the prospective client on the benefits and limitations of the product.
▪ Give a detailed explanation on the options within the product.
▪ Give a summary of the reasons why the product is being recommended.
▪ Explain the benefit illustration and highlight the guaranteed and nonguaranteed benefits (if applicable).
▪ Disclose any distribution costs, charges and expenses under the policy. When the reviews for prospective client are necessary?
▪ A change in the client’s personal circumstances, such as the birth of a child.
▪ External developments, such as changes in the CPF ruling which can have an impact on the client’s financial position and/or the appropriateness of products already held.
▪ Original products purchased by the client are not adequate to cover all his need.
▪ Launching of new products in the financial services market.
paragraphs.textJP:
顧客の長期的なニーズを最優先に考え、顧客との関係を継続的なものにしてください。顧客が十分な医療保障を持っている場合は、複数の保険証券では給付金を重複して請求できない可能性があるため、追加の健康保険証券を推奨しないでください。

【第14章 重要概念まとめ】

ニーズ販売 vs プロダクト販売
ニーズ販売がプロダクト販売より望ましい理由：①サービス志向、②圧力販売ではない、③関係性に基づく。

事実確認（ファクトファインディング）とは：見込み顧客の個人的状況・財務・将来の目標などに関する質問への回答を収集するプロセス。

Fact-Find Documentの各セクションの目的：
▪ 顧客への重要なお知らせ：保険外交員の情報、代理する保険会社、提案できる商品クラスを顧客に伝える。正確な情報提供の重要性を強調する。
▪ 申請タイプ：開示の種類、申請タイプ1・2・3。
▪ 個人情報：会社の管理目的。必要な商品タイプの予備的評価。年齢情報は引受と保険料算定に必須。
▪ 職業詳細：障害所得保険（DII）の適格性。DIIの待機期間の設定。職業上のリスクの有無。支払能力。
▪ 配偶者・扶養家族の情報：請求時の識別、保険料算定、職業上のリスク（配偶者）、扶養家族としての適格性（A&H保険に年齢制限がある場合）、扶養家族の保障の必要性。
▪ 既存健康保険/既存ポートフォリオ：既存プランが傷病・障害時のニーズをどの程度カバーするかを示す。
▪ 個人の優先事項と目的：顧客が必要とするA&H保険の種類に関する優先順位を把握する。
▪ 健康状態：被保険者が保険に加入できるか、またはどのような条件で加入できるかを判断する。
▪ 外国人労働者向け医療保険：S-Pass/就労パス保有者のために購入する場合、Ministry of Manpowerの強化された医療保険要件への対応を確認する。
▪ 証券の切替：顧客の回答が「はい」の場合、証券切替の影響を説明する。
▪ 顧客の財務状況：推奨プランの支払能力の確認と財務ニーズ計画の策定を支援する。
▪ アドバイザーの宣言：Fact-Find Documentの情報を厳重に機密として扱い、適切な保険商品の特定のみに使用すること（Personal Data Protection Act 2012の要件を念頭に置く）の宣言。代理人と顧客の信頼関係の確立。

Fact-Find Documentで顧客のニーズを特定する際の質問例：
▪ 緊急資金はあるか？
▪ 雇用状況と職業は？
▪ ライフステージは？
▪ 扶養家族はいるか？
▪ 本人・扶養家族が個人生命・健康保険やグループ医療保険（グループCIや入院・外科手術保険等）に加入しているか？
▪ 生命・健康保険の必要性があるか？
▪ 提案する保険証券の保険料を支払えるか？
▪ 優先事項は正しく述べられているか？

緊急資金の重要性：家計の主な収入源の失業や短期的な障害による収入途絶に備える。

ライフステージと障害・疾病からの収入保護の必要性：
▪ 就労期間中は、傷害や長期疾患による障害から収入を守ることが重要。
▪ 傷病への備えはライフステージを問わずすべての人に必要。
▪ 高齢者は傷病リスクが高いため、CI保険・医療費保険・長期ケア保険のニーズが高い。
▪ 扶養家族がいる場合は、重大疾病や障害に備えた扶養家族の医療費のための手当てが必要。

各保険タイプへの対応方法：
▪ 障害所得保険（DII）：現在の収入との対比で保障額が十分かを確認。目標退職年齢までのカバレッジ・待機期間・雇用主からのDII給付があるかを確認。Limitation Clauseによる過剰保険に注意。
▪ 高度障害（TPD）給付付き生命保険：既存の生命保険のTPD給付を確認。
▪ 医療費保険（MEI）：入院病院・病室のタイプ・年間・生涯限度額・co-insurance・deductibleなどを確認。有効期限・更新条件を確認。Co-ordination of Benefit Clauseを念頭に置く。
▪ CI保険：単独証券か他の証券のライダーかを確認。保険者の上限合計保険金額に注意。
▪ 傷害保険：除外事項・制限を含む保障範囲を確認。
▪ 長期ケア保険：給付内容・給付発動条件・ADLの定義・必要ADL数を確認。実費払戻タイプか確認。
▪ 入院見舞金保険：1日あたりの給付金と給付日数の上限を確認。
▪ 生命保険・従業員給付：会社の保険への加入状況・生命保険の有無を確認。

障害所得保護ニーズの定量化：
▪ 方法1：月収の75%が障害時の収入保護ニーズ。
▪ 方法2：収入ではなく月次支出を使用して必要保障額を算出。
▪ 方法3：高度障害（TPD）時に一時金給付を提供する方法。

医療費・付帯費用の定量化：最も一般的な入院原因、治療費、希望する病院・病室タイプ、初日からの保障の必要性、退職後の保険料継続支払能力について顧客と協議する。

入院見舞金ニーズの定量化：必要な保護水準 ＝ 月次総費用 − 既存の入院見舞金（月額）

CI保険の定量化：計算は科学よりも技術的側面が強い。考慮すべき要素：最もかかりやすい重大疾病（家族の病歴が参考になる）、家族のニーズと費用、家族の依存度、MEIとDIIの充足度、希望する医療水準（私立か公立病院）、追加給付型か加速給付型のCI保険か（追加給付型は65歳で保障終了となることが多いが、加速給付型は通常終身生命保険に付加されるため終身保障）、早期診断給付・複数請求・糖尿病や精神疾患などの追加保障が必要か、顧客の好みと納得感。

商品推奨前の基本原則：
▪ 必要な場合にのみ商品を推奨する。
▪ 顧客の状況に最も適した商品のみを推奨する。
▪ 代理する会社が取り扱っていない商品が必要な場合、顧客に代替品を探せるよう告知する。

商品理解のための質問例：
▪ 商品ラインナップは？
▪ 商品の目的（費用払戻か収入補償か）は？
▪ 請求の適格要件は？
▪ 待機期間はあるか？期間と発効日は？
▪ 更新条件（保証更新・任意更新・条件付き更新・解約可能）は？
▪ 除外事項・制限は？
▪ CI保険の生存期間・DII保険の職業変更通知義務などの特別条項は？
▪ Co-ordination of Benefit Clauseはあるか？
▪ 免責（deductible）やco-insurance条項はあるか？
▪ 1日・1年・生涯あたりの限度額は？
▪ 給付金の支払時期・方法は？
▪ 販売コスト・手数料・費用は？
▪ 保障期間は？
▪ 保険料・支払頻度・変更可能性は？
▪ 保険料の支払猶予期間は？
▪ 保険料不払いの場合の影響は？
▪ 医的・非医的限度額（健康診断が必要となる引受ガイドライン）は？
▪ 失効防止オプションがある証券タイプは？
▪ 地理的な保障範囲の制限はあるか？

商品選択における支払能力の重要性：
▪ 支払能力は最も適した商品を選ぶ際の重要な要素。
▪ ほとんどの証券は定期的な保険料支払いが必要で、最初の保険料が支払えても継続できるとは限らない。
▪ 早期失効は顧客・保険外交員・保険者の誰にとっても利益にならない。

証券選択のポイント：
▪ 不足分のみをカバーする新証券を推奨して過剰保険を最小化する。
▪ できる限り長い保障期間を確保する。
▪ 長期的な保険料支払能力を確認する。
▪ 過剰保険にならないようにする。
▪ 正しい被保険者に保険をかける。
▪ 扶養家族がいる場合は十分な死亡保障があるかを確認する。
▪ ライダーで保険料節約になる場合は推奨する。
▪ CI保険の追加給付型と加速給付型の2種類を確認する。
▪ 有効期限・保険料の差・既存生命保険がある場合のCI特約（Waiver of Premium）について顧客と検討する。
▪ 他の生命保険保護がある顧客にはCI Waiver of Premium特約を検討する。
▪ 雇用主提供MEIに加入している顧客が追加MEIを必要とする場合は、雇用主提供プランを補完する商品を確認する。
▪ 推奨商品の代替プランも確認する。

見込み顧客への推奨内容説明プロセス例：
▪ 商品の目的を述べる。▪ 商品の性質を説明する。▪ 給付内容と制限を説明する。▪ 商品内オプションを詳説する。▪ 推奨理由の概要を説明する。▪ 給付イラストを説明し、確定・不確定給付を強調する。▪ 販売コスト・手数料・費用を開示する。

定期的な顧客見直しが必要なタイミング：
▪ 子供の誕生などの個人的状況の変化。
▪ CPF制度変更などの外部環境の変化。
▪ 当初商品がすべてのニーズを満たすのに不十分になった場合。
▪ 金融サービス市場での新商品の発売。

chapters.title: CASE STUDIES
learningOutcomes: apply the principles that you have learnt from the previous chapters so as to carry out a proper needs-based sales advisory process through looking at two case scenarios

paragraphs.text: In this chapter, we bring you through two case studies to illustrate how the whole needs-based sales process may be carried out.
paragraphs.textJP: この章では、ニーズに基づく販売プロセス全体がどのように実施されるかを示す2つのケーススタディをご紹介します。

paragraphs.text: The examples given here cannot be used as a comprehensive guide for every situation. You should also keep abreast of the LIA and GIA Guidelines On Disclosure Requirements and Needs-Based Sales Process for A&H Insurance Products. 2. CASE STUDY 1 – INDIVIDUAL HEALTH INSURANCE
paragraphs.textJP:
ここで示す事例はすべての状況に対応する包括的なガイドとして使用することはできません。また、LIAおよびGIAの「A&H保険商品に関する開示要件とニーズに基づく販売プロセスに関するガイドライン」を常に把握しておく必要があります。

2. ケーススタディ1 — 個人健康保険

paragraphs.text: Angela has done a fact-find with Tommy Tang, her new client, who is a Singapore Citizen. Below is a summary of the data that she has extracted from the Fact-Find Document: Mr Tang : 35 years old Mrs Tang : 35 years old, housewife Son : 5 years old Daughter : 1 year old
paragraphs.textJP:
アンジェラは新規顧客のトミー・タン（シンガポール市民）と事実確認を実施しました。以下は彼女がFact-Find Documentから収集したデータの概要です。
タン氏：35歳
タン夫人：35歳、専業主婦
息子：5歳
娘：1歳

paragraphs.text:
Existing Health Insurance Policies:
▪ Group Hospital & Surgical (H & S) Insurance (Plan A) (See Appendix 15A)
paragraphs.textJP:
既存の健康保険証券：
▪ グループ入院・外科手術（H&S）保険（プランA）（付録15A参照）

paragraphs.text: Angela has also obtained the following information from Tommy: (a) Mr and Mrs Tang have sufficient cover for death protection. (b) His family’s average monthly expenditure is S$3,000 and his income is S$6,000. (c) He has not set aside any funds for protection planning, other than his savings which is intended to meet any emergency needs, such as hospitalisation. (d) He would like to have a Critical Illness Insurance policy of S$200,000 for himself and S$100,000 for his wife. (e) He thinks that he will need maintenance cost for 21 years for his wife in the event of his disability, as the youngest child should be independent by then. (f) The estimated expenses required for the children in the event of Mrs Tang’s disability are S$1,500 per month. (g) He and his family would stay in the A ward of a public hospital should they be hospitalised. (h) His budget for Health Insurance cover is S$700 per month.
paragraphs.textJP: アンジェラはさらにトミーから以下の情報を得ました。（a）タン夫妻には死亡保障として十分なカバレッジがある。（b）家族の平均月次支出はS$3,000で、収入はS$6,000である。（c）入院などの緊急ニーズに備えた貯蓄のほかは、保障計画のための資金を別途確保していない。（d）自身にS$200,000、妻にS$100,000のCI保険を希望している。（e）末子が自立するまでの21年間、障害が生じた場合に妻の生活費が必要と考えている。（f）タン夫人が障害を負った場合に子供たちに必要な推定費用は月S$1,500である。（g）入院の場合は公立病院のAワードを希望する。（h）健康保険の予算は月S$700である。

paragraphs.text: Having gathered the necessary information, Angela then proceeds to analyse the insurance needs of Tommy and his family members.
paragraphs.textJP: 必要な情報を収集したのち、アンジェラはトミーとその家族のメンバーの保険ニーズの分析を進めます。

paragraphs.text: The most suitable policy for maintenance cost is Disability Income Insurance.
paragraphs.textJP: 維持費用に最も適した保険証券は障害所得保険です。

paragraphs.text: Tommy does not have a Disability Income Insurance policy. He has indicated in his Fact-Find Document that he has an urgent need for cover for loss of income resulting from accident or sickness, as he is the sole breadwinner in his family.
paragraphs.textJP: Tommyは障害所得保険証券を持っていません。ファクトファインド文書で、家族の唯一の稼ぎ手であるため、事故または疾病による所得喪失に対する補償が緊急に必要であると記載しています。

paragraphs.text: As such, Angela proposes a Disability Income Insurance policy with benefit at 75% of Tommy’s monthly salary, i.e. S$4,500 and an escalation benefit of 5%. This will ensure that the family is adequately provided for, in the event of Tommy’s disability. The deferred period will be six months, as the employer will continue to pay Tommy’s salary up to six months in the event that he is disabled. Mrs Tang, a housewife, is not eligible for Disability Income Insurance. In her case, there are two possible ways to provide her with protection against disability, i.e. through the Total and Permanent Disability (TPD) Benefit under the Life Insurance policies, or through a Personal Accident Insurance policy.
paragraphs.textJP: そこでAngelaは、Tommyの月給の75%（すなわちS$4,500）の給付と年率5%のエスカレーション給付を持つ障害所得保険証券を提案します。これにより、Tommyの障害時に家族が十分に養われることが保証されます。据置期間は6ヶ月とします。雇用主がTommyの障害時に最大6ヶ月間給与の支払いを継続するためです。Tang夫人もパートタイムで働いているため、夫人にも所得保護が必要です。Angelaは、Tang夫人のパートタイム収入S$1,500の75%であるS$1,125の月額給付の障害所得保険証券を推奨します。

paragraphs.text: The amount of TPD cover which she requires is as follows: Maintenance Costs Required On Mrs Tang’s Disablement Yearly income needed Number of years income is needed Expected annual rate of return from funds (adjusted for inflation) Estimated funds required to provide income Less Funds available Existing Insurance Funds required : S$1,500 (current monthly expenditure) x 12 = S$18,000 : 21 years : 3% (assume that rate of return is 5%, and inflation rate is 2%) : S$18,000 x 15.8775 (refer to Table A2 at end of this Study Guide) = S$285,795 : S$0 : S$285,795
paragraphs.textJP:
Tang夫人の障害時に必要なTPD補償額は以下のとおりです：
維持費用：S$1,500（現在の月間支出）× 12 = 年間S$18,000
所得が必要な年数：21年
資金の期待年間収益率（インフレ調整後）：3%
所得を提供するために必要な推定資金：S$18,000 × 年金現価係数
控除：利用可能な資金、既存の保険
必要な資金：推定総額 − 既存資金

paragraphs.text: Having determined the maintenance costs for the Tangs, Angela then goes on to determine whether the Tangs’ current Medical Expense Insurance policies are sufficient to meet their needs.
paragraphs.textJP: Tang家の維持費用を決定した後、AngelaはTang家の現在の医療費保険証券が十分であるかどうかを判断します。

paragraphs.text: The policies that may be used to meet medical costs are: (a) Medical Expense Insurance; (b) Managed Healthcare Insurance; (c) Long-Term Care Insurance; (d) Critical Illness Insurance; (e) Hospital Cash Insurance; and (f) Personal Accident policy with medical expense benefit.
paragraphs.textJP:
医療費を賄うために使用できる保険証券は以下のとおりです：
(a) 医療費保険
(b) マネージドヘルスケア保険
(c) 長期介護保険
(d) 重大疾病保険
(e) 入院日額保険

paragraphs.text: According to the fact-find results, Tommy only has Medical Expense Insurance coverage under his employer’s insurance. Angela informed Tommy that Group H&S is not portable and that he may lose it later or the coverage may change if he moves into a different job or retire. They agreed to consider Group H&S as a temporary (not lifetime) benefit instead. She then proceeds to determine the coverage he requires for Medical Expense Insurance policies to meet the Tangs’ needs in the event of hospitalisation.
paragraphs.textJP: ファクトファインドの結果によると、Tommyは雇用主の保険の下でのみ医療費保険の補償を持っています。Angelaは、団体H&S保険はポータブルではなく、転職や退職時に失う可能性があること、また補償内容が変更される可能性があることをTommyに伝えました。そこで団体H&S保険は（生涯の給付ではなく）一時的な給付とみなすことに合意しました。Angelaは、Tommyが個人として必要とする補償を決定する作業に進みます。

paragraphs.text: Angela went through the following to recommend a suitable Medical Expense Insurance: (a) Confirm his preference of stay in the event of hospitalisation. (b) Briefly compute what his Group H&S would have covered him in a specific medical condition to provide a view if it can cover a portion of co-insurance and deductible of a hospitalisation for as long as the GHS is valid. (c) Present a medical expense insurance plan that will provide cover for Public Ward A hospitalisation, showing the current and future annual premiums of the proposed plan. Please refer to the insurer’s website for the latest premium rates. (d) Tommy was informed that: (i) Premium rate is non-guaranteed. With medical inflation, the insurer will update the current premium table periodically and premium will get more expensive in the future. This is to give a practical view of the future affordability of premium on the chosen plan. (ii) While the benefits payable under Group H&S can be used to cover part of co-insurance and deductible of the average bill size of the medical condition, Medical Expense Insurance and Group H&S are both subject to the Co-ordination of Benefits Clause. Hence it may not be necessary to have first dollar cover for his individual medical expense insurance. This way, he can save some premiums to fund for future premiums. (iii) Once he loses the Group H&S, his co-payments for any hospitalisation has to be made out-of-pocket. (iv) Tommy acknowledged this and agreed to go with a plan that has deductible and co-insurance in order to lower the premiums.
paragraphs.textJP:
Angelaは適切な医療費保険を推奨するために以下の手順を踏みました：
(a) 入院時の希望する病棟の確認。Tommyは公立病院のB1クラス病棟を希望
(b) 団体H&Sが特定の疾患においてどの程度カバーするか簡単に計算し、H&Sが有効な間、共同保険と免責額の一部をカバーできるか確認
(c) Tommyの希望する病棟タイプに合わせた医療費保険の提示。MediShield Lifeとの連携、Integrated Shield Planの選択肢を含む
(d) 共同保険と免責額の自己負担について説明。Tommyは毎年S$3,500の年間免責額を自己保険することに同意
(e) MediSaveからの保険料支払いの選択肢を説明
(f) 提案するIntegrated Shield PlanのB1プラン詳細：MediShield Lifeの基本補償に加え、B1クラス病棟の補償、プロレーション適用、年間補償限度額、生涯補償限度額
(g) 保険料の見積もり：Tommyの年齢帯に基づく年間保険料をMediSaveから支払い可能であることを確認

paragraphs.text: The discussion with Tommy on affordability should not only be on the present, but also the future sustainability to pay premiums, whether with Medisave or cash.
paragraphs.textJP: Tommyとの手頃さについての議論は、現在だけでなく、MediSaveまたは現金で保険料を将来的に持続的に支払えるかどうかについても行うべきです。

paragraphs.text: The Tangs also require Critical Illness Insurance cover, as the medical expenses incurred in the event of a critical illness may not be fully covered by Tommy’s policies. They will also need Long-Term Care Insurance cover (under ElderShield) if they can afford the premium, as the cover is not insured by his two existing policies.
paragraphs.textJP: Tang家は重大疾病保険の補償も必要です。重大疾病が発生した場合の医療費はTommyの保険証券では全額補償されない可能性があるためです。また、保険料を支払う余裕がある場合は、既存の2つの保険証券では補償されていない長期介護保険の補償（ElderShieldの下で）も必要になります。

paragraphs.text: As the Tangs already have policies for protection against premature death, Angela proposed a 100% Acceleration Benefit type of Critical Illness Insurance policy packaged with a Whole Life Insurance policy, so that they can have the cover for life. This policy will also take care of Mrs Tang’s TPD needs. To summarise, the package for Mr and Mrs Tang is shown in Table 15.1 below: Table 15.1: Summary Of Insurance Package For Mr & Mrs Tang Mr Tang (Tommy) Type Of Plan & Sum Assured Monthly Premium Enhance Income Shield Advantage Plan S$500.63/year and Fully funded by Medisave 100% Acceleration Benefit Type of Critical Illness Insurance policy packaged with Whole Life Insurance policy - S$200,000 S$380.00 Disability Income Insurance of S$4,500, with a 5% escalation and six months’ deferred period, up to the age of 65 years S$125.50 Total Monthly Premium with cash S$505.50 Mrs Tang Type Of Plan & Sum Assured Monthly Premium Enhance Income Shield Advantage Plan S$500.63/year and Fully funded by Medisave 100% Acceleration Benefit Type of Critical Illness Insurance policy packaged with Whole Life Insurance policy - S$100,000 S$180.00 Personal Accident (PA) Insurance policy - S$50,000 S$10.00 Total Monthly Premium with cash S$190.00 Two Children Type Of Plan & Sum Assured Monthly Premium Enhance Income Shield Advantage Plan S$175.63/year per child and Fully funded by Medisave Source of IncomeShield Premium: https://www.income.com.sg/forms/general/enhanced-incomeshieldmain- premium-table
paragraphs.textJP:
Tang家は既に早すぎる死亡に対する保護の保険証券を有しているため、Angelaは終身保険証券にパッケージ化した100%前払給付型の重大疾病保険を提案し、生涯の補償を確保しました。この保険証券はTang夫人のTPDニーズにも対応します。

Tang夫妻のパッケージの要約（表15.1）：

Tommy Tang：
・Integrated Shield Plan（B1プラン）— MediSave支払い
・障害所得保険（月額S$4,500、6ヶ月据置）— 月額保険料S$125.50
・終身保険 + CI前払給付ライダー — 月額保険料S$190.00

Tang夫人：
・障害所得保険（月額S$1,125、3ヶ月据置）— 月額保険料S$50.00
・終身保険 + CI前払給付ライダー + TPD給付 — 月額保険料S$140.00

合計月額保険料：S$505.50（現金払い分）

paragraphs.text: The total premium payable with cash works out to be S$695.50 (i.e. S$505.50 + S$190.00) and is within Tommy’s S$700 budget. As there is no excess premium for the Long-Term Care Insurance cover, Angela has decided not to propose it at this moment.
paragraphs.textJP: 現金での支払保険料合計はS$695.50（S$505.50 + S$190.00）となり、TommyのS$700の予算内です。長期介護保険の超過保険料がないため、Angelaはこの時点では提案しないことにしました。

paragraphs.text:
Having decided on the types of policies that are suitable to Tommy, Angela next proceeds to complete the “Representative Recommendations” section of the Fact-Find Document. REPRESENTATIVE’S RECOMMENDATIONS Please note that the information that you provide will be the basis on which advice will be given. Please also note that any inaccurate or incomplete information provided may affect the suitability of the recommendations presented to you. Client’s Objective(s) Client’s concern, investment objectives, shortfall amount ($), time horizon, investment risk profile, where applicable He likes to have a Critical Illness Insurance policy of S$200,000 for himself and S$100,000 for his wife. REPRESENTATIVE’S RECOMMENDATIONS Plan(s) Recommendation Full name of plan and rider, duration, sum assured ($), premium ($)/frequency, fund name(s), investment amount and respective risk classification, where applicable Reason(s) for Recommendations
▪ State how the plan meets the Client’s need(s).
▪ State and explain features and benefits relating to the product sold. Risk / Limitation(s) of Plan
▪ State any possible risks relating to the product sold.
▪ State possible disadvantage(s) based on circumstances of the Client.  Medical Expense Insurance Proposed Enhanced IncomeShield Advantage Plan for the entire family which are fully payable with Medisave. This plan provides cover for up to Public Hospital Ward A in the event of hospitalisation. There is co-insurance and deductible on this policy to make the premium affordable.  Critical Illness Insurance Policy Tommy Proposed a 100% Acceleration Benefit Type of Critical Illness Insurance cover packaged with a Whole Life Insurance policy – sum assured of S$200,000 as specified by Tommy. This policy is suitable for Tommy’s budget. It will provide him with death, disability and critical illness coverage. It serves to supplement the Whole Life Insurance policy in providing for the family, in the event of his early demise. Reason(s) for Deviation(s) Reasons for any deviation from client’s profile, objectives and/or representative’s recommendations (where applicable)
▪ Premiums are more than Client’s budget
▪ Funds recommended (e.g. ILP sub-fund, par fund) are of a higher risk than client’s risk preference Client’s choice of product/funds differs from representative’s recommended plan/funds Furthermore, it provides life cover for him, ensuring that he is covered in his old age. Mrs Tang Proposed a 100% Acceleration Benefit Type of Critical Illness Insurance cover packaged with a Whole Life Insurance policy – sum assured of S$100,000 as specified by Mr Tang. This policy will give Mrs Tang the additional TPD cover that she needs. It will also provide her with life cover, thus ensuring that she will be covered in her old age when her need is the greatest. REPRESENTATIVE’S RECOMMENDATIONS  Disability Expense Protection Tommy Proposed a Disability Income Insurance policy of S$4,500 (75% of salary), with 5% escalation and a six months’ deferred period, up to the age of 65 years This policy is considered suitable for Tommy, because it will provide his family with a monthly income in the event that he is totally or partially disabled. Mrs Tang Has not proposed Disability Income Insurance policy, as she is not working and, hence, not eligible. Has proposed a PA Insurance policy of S$50,000 to make up the shortfall for TPD cover after she takes up the S$100,000 Critical Illness Insurance policy. This policy is recommended, as it will provide her with cover for death, TPD and medical expenses at a very low cost. This means that this policy may also help to take care of the shortfall in Medical Expense Insurance cover.
paragraphs.textJP:
Tommyに適した保険証券の種類を決定した後、Angelaはファクトファインド文書の「担当者の推奨（Representative's Recommendations）」セクションの記入に進みます。

このセクションには以下が含まれます：
・顧客の財務ニーズの要約
・推奨する保険証券の詳細（保険証券名、保険者名、保険金額、保険料、支払い方法）
・推奨の理由
・顧客が認識すべき重要事項（待機期間、免責事項、共同保険等）
・推奨が顧客の予算内であることの確認
・推奨される保険料の内訳と支払い方法の選択肢
・顧客と担当者の両方の署名

Angelaの推奨書にはTommy Tang夫妻について以下が記載されます：
・Tommyの所得保護ニーズ（障害所得保険S$4,500/月）
・Tang夫人の所得保護ニーズ（障害所得保険S$1,125/月）
・医療費保護ニーズ（Integrated Shield Plan B1）
・重大疾病保護ニーズ（終身保険+CI前払給付）
・各保険証券の保険料と支払い方法

paragraphs.text: Having finalised the insurance package to be recommended to the client, Angela then arranges to meet up with his client, and presents the package to him.
paragraphs.textJP: 顧客に推奨する保険パッケージを確定した後、Angelaは顧客との面談を手配し、パッケージを提示します。

paragraphs.text: The case study illustrates one of the possible sets of recommendations among others. As a result, certain plausible suggestions have not been discussed, such as extending the client’s coverage to include accidental loss of limbs, or medical expenses arising from accidents.
paragraphs.textJP: このケーススタディは、他の可能な推奨の中の1つのセットを示しています。結果として、事故による四肢の喪失補償や事故に起因する医療費など、一部の妥当な提案は議論されていません。

paragraphs.text: As an insurance representative, you should also be aware of “Your Guide To Health Insurance”, as well as the “Evaluating My Health Insurance Coverage” sheet issued by LIA in conjunction with the MoneySENSE national financial education programme. You can view and download them from the LIA Website at: www.lia.org.sg
paragraphs.textJP: 保険担当者として、「Your Guide To Health Insurance」および「Evaluating My Health Insurance Coverage」についても認識しておくべきです。

paragraphs.text: You should bring these documents to the attention of your prospective client and discuss them with him. 3. CASE STUDY 2 – GROUP HEALTH INSURANCE
paragraphs.textJP:
これらの文書を見込み客に紹介し、話し合うべきです。
3. ケーススタディ2 — 団体健康保険

paragraphs.text: We will now look at another case study focusing on a prospective client’s need for Group Health Insurance. Apex Services Company Private Limited has approached you for quotations on Group Term Life and Health Insurance policies to cover its employees as part of its employee benefit package. As the Company wants the policies to be tailored to their needs, you will have to get the Company to complete the Group Insurance Fact-Finding (GIFF) Form. Note that the format of the Form may vary for different insurers.
paragraphs.textJP: 次に、見込み客の団体健康保険のニーズに焦点を当てた別のケーススタディを見てみましょう。Apex Services Company Private Limitedは、従業員の福利厚生パッケージの一環として、従業員をカバーする団体定期生命保険および健康保険証券の見積もりを求めて、あなたにアプローチしてきました。会社はニーズに合わせた保険証券を希望しているため、会社にGroup Insurance Fact-Finding（GIFF）フォームに記入してもらう必要があります。

paragraphs.text: Before proceeding further, you need to find out whether the Company (i.e. the prospective client) meets your insurer’s underwriting requirements in terms of group size and group stability, etc.
paragraphs.textJP: 先に進む前に、会社（すなわち見込み客）が保険者の引受要件（グループの規模やその他の適格基準）を満たしているかどうかを確認する必要があります。

paragraphs.text: Once you have established that the prospective client is eligible for the group coverage, you may then proceed to explain to your prospective client the following: (a) the types of policies available and the benefits offered; (b) the advantages of joining the Portable Medical Benefits Scheme, Transferable Medical Insurance Scheme or providing a shield plan for employees, as well as how these schemes work; and (c) the differences between compulsory and voluntary participation, as well as their advantages.
paragraphs.textJP:
見込み客が団体補償の適格性を確立したら、以下について見込み客に説明することに進みます：
(a) 利用可能な保険証券の種類と提供される給付
(b) Portable Medical Benefits Scheme（PMBS）、Transferable Medical Insurance Scheme（TMIS）への加入の利点、または従業員にシールドプランを提供する利点、およびこれらのスキームの仕組み
(c) 各カテゴリーの従業員の保険金額と補償の決定

paragraphs.text: In addition, you will need to help your prospective client to determine the amount of sum assured for each category of employees. If your prospective client has an existing plan, they may wish to stick to the same amount of benefit, or make some minor adjustments to it. For a prospective client buying the Group Term Life Insurance for the first time, it is your responsibility to help the prospective client to determine the sum assured. There are two common methods which can be used, i.e.: (a) according to rank; or (b) number of times of the employee’s basic salary.
paragraphs.textJP: さらに、各カテゴリーの従業員の保険金額を決定するのを支援する必要があります。見込み客が既存のプランを持っている場合、同額の給付を維持するか、若干の調整を行うことを希望する場合があります。初めて団体定期生命保険を購入する見込み客の場合、各カテゴリーの従業員の適切な補償額を決定するのを支援するのはあなたの責任です。金額を決定するには、ランキング方式と給与の倍数方式の2つの方法があります。

paragraphs.text: The first method is easier to manage, as it goes according to the rank of the employee. However, it does not take into account the length of service of the staff concerned.
paragraphs.textJP: 最初の方法は、従業員の役職に基づくため管理が容易です。ただし、従業員の勤続年数や個人的な貢献は考慮されません。

paragraphs.text: For example, a new staff joining as an executive will get the same amount of benefit as one who has been with the company for two years, although his pay may be lower than the more experienced staff. The second method, on the other hand, takes into account the length of service, as the salary of a more experienced staff is likely to be higher than that of a new staff. Hence, this method may be more equitable as compared to the former method. You may also ask for the client’s budget, as this will also help to determine the sum assured to be covered.
paragraphs.textJP: 例えば、エグゼクティブとして入社した新しいスタッフは、より経験のあるスタッフと同額の給与を受け取っていなくても、2年間勤務した者と同じ給付を受けます。一方、第2の方法（給与の倍数方式）は勤続年数を考慮します。より経験のあるスタッフの給与は新しいスタッフよりも高い可能性が高いためです。したがって、この方法は各従業員の個別の状況をより正確に反映するため、より公平である場合があります。

paragraphs.text: For instance, in this case, your prospective client has told you that the Company prefers to go by the ranking method. Based on the budget given, you can then design a plan, such as the one below: Category Sum Assured Per Person Term Assurance Living Assurance Senior Management S$500,000 S$250,000 Managers/Executives S$250,000 S$125,000 Assistant Executives S$100,000 S$50,000 Clerks S$50,000 S$25,000
paragraphs.textJP:
このケースでは、見込み客は会社がランキング方式を好むと述べています。与えられた予算に基づいて、以下のようなプランを設計できます：

カテゴリー | 1人あたりの保険金額（定期保険） | 1人あたりの保険金額（生存保険）
上級管理職 | S$500,000 | S$250,000
マネージャー/エグゼクティブ | S$250,000 | S$125,000
アシスタント・エグゼクティブ | S$100,000 | S$50,000
事務員 | S$50,000 | S$25,000

paragraphs.text: By attaching 50% Acceleration Critical Illness Insurance cover as a rider to the Group Term Life Insurance policy, it will help your prospective client to provide the benefit at an affordable amount (if there is a constraint in the amount that the Company can spend).
paragraphs.textJP: 団体定期生命保険証券にライダーとして50%前払重大疾病保険を付帯することで、見込み客が手頃な金額で（会社の支出額に制約がある場合に）従業員に重大疾病の給付を提供するのに役立ちます。

paragraphs.text: For the Group Hospital & Surgical Insurance policy, you can also plan according to the ranking method as follows: Category Plan Senior Management Plan 1 – Room & Board S$450 Managers/Executives Plan 2 – Room & Board S$300 Others Plan 3 – Room & Board S$200
paragraphs.textJP:
団体入院・手術保険証券については、以下のようにランキング方式で計画することもできます：
カテゴリー / プラン
上級管理職 / プランA
管理職 / プランB
一般従業員 / プランC

paragraphs.text: Next, obtain the Company’s past three years of claims history and list of probable incurred but not yet reported claims. The claims history is very important, as this will determine the premium to be charged.
paragraphs.textJP: 次に、会社の過去3年間の請求履歴と、発生したがまだ報告されていない請求の見込みリストを取得します。請求履歴は、更新保険料の算定に非常に重要です。

paragraphs.text: Note also that your prospective client must duly complete and sign the GIFF Form. It is important that you sign this Form too. The insurer is not allowed to give any quotation, unless this Form has been duly completed and signed. Having completed the GIFF Form, submit it to the insurer to work out a quotation for your presentation to the prospective client.
paragraphs.textJP: また、見込み客がGIFFフォームに正式に記入し署名しなければならないことにご留意ください。このフォームにはあなたも署名することが重要です。このフォームが正式に記入され署名されない限り、保険者は見積もりを提供することは認められません。GIFFフォームの記入が完了したら、見込み客への提示のための見積もりを作成するよう保険者に提出してください。

paragraphs.text: The sales quotation will include the coverage, underwriting guidelines, premium chargeable, exclusions and waiting periods, etc. You have to go through the quotation and highlight the important points that your prospective client needs to be aware of, e.g. exclusions, limitations, restricted provisions and conditions, as well as no free-look period applicable to any Group Insurance policy.
paragraphs.textJP: 見積書には、補償範囲、引受ガイドライン、保険料、免責事項、待機期間等が含まれます。見積書を確認し、見込み客が認識すべき重要な点を強調する必要があります。例えば、免責事項、制限事項、制限付き条項および条件、ならびに団体保険証券にはクーリングオフ期間が適用されないことなどです。
見込み客が見積書を受諾したら、必要に応じて申込書と健康申告書の記入を支援し、保険者に提出する必要があります。
